title: Forgiving
verse: “And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.” - (Ephesians 4:32)
content:
Paul gives the Ephesians general exhortation by which to live.

Every day we are in need of forgiveness because we sin and God graciously forgives us every time; therefore forgiving others is to be reciprocated.

The Christian way of life is to be courteous, kind, and compassionate to everyone, this is the way to treat each other regardless of how they treat us.  

Ophelia is one of the most compassionate people you’ll meet this side of heaven, if you ask her about it she’ll smile shyly and say, “God has forgiven me so I am most grateful, it makes me understanding.”

We can relate.

title: The Power of God
verse: “For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.” - (1 Corinthians 1:18)
content:
Paul’s declaration to the people of Corinth, that the power of God and wisdom are enveloped in Christ.

Susan remembered like it was just yesterday, the day she received Christ into her heart.  It was a magnificent, extraordinary day, she felt and saw the power of God in her life.  She was miraculously healed and delivered from drug abuse.  She is happy and free and so very thankful for the message of the cross.

Bertha, Susan’s friend refuses to come to Christ, Susan continues to pray, she knows the power of God, and it works for all of us.

title: Celebration of the Somber
verse: “Blessed are they who mourn: for they shall be comforted.”  (Matthew 5:4)
content: When your heart breaks over personal loss, there is nothing that seemingly satisfies than having your loved one back. It seems ironic then that Jesus said mourners will be comforted, He said it because He knows that our hearts will rejoice, we will see our loved ones again—if we and they have trusted Christ as Savior. This blessed promise should make us think seriously about death that awaits us all. We should live in such a way that we and others are secure in the future by sharing the Gospel. Share it today and every day so that there will be a celebration of the somber and joyful reunions in Heaven.

title: Take Your Place
verse: “People will come from east and west and north and south, and will take their places at the feast in the kingdom of God.  Indeed there are those who are last who will be first, and first who will be last." - (Luke 13:29-30)
content:
A portion of the parables of the Kingdom that Jesus told.

It matters not when you came to know Christ, or which nation you came from, what is important is that you came and took your place.

Accepting Jesus is the best decision one can make, it is life transforming.  This I am sure you can attest to. The benefits are enormous; the joy and peace you now have are worth every ounce of taking the stand for Christ and the best is yet to come!

title: Fearfully and Wonderfully Made
verse: “For you created my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother's womb.” - (Psalm 139:13)
content:
David talks about God’s perfect knowledge of man whom He made.

A masterpiece, work of art, wonder gem, feather in God’s cap, wonder, prize you are. You can’t be cloned, no one else is just like, your DNA is the only one there is.  Every intricate detail about you was planned before your mother met your father. So regardless of what you’ve heard, what they’ve said about and to you, God made you SPECIAL.

He plotted the course of your life and knew the path that you would choose, where you would be born, and the experiences you would have.

You are fearfully and wonderfully made, live like it!

title: Hospitable Housewives
verse: “But God, who comforts the downcast, comforted us by the coming of Titus.”  (2 Corinthians 7:6)
content: Women have a yearning for being close to people; this mirrors the ministry of the Holy Spirit. The simple act of just being near may be all it takes to bring a person through one of their darkest trials. For example, Paul writes that Titus’ visit and good report about what God was producing in the Corinthians, was the medicine he needed; it reassured him of God’s faithful, fruitful ministry. It assured Paul that his labor and suffering was not in vain. Perhaps, you feel you can’t do anything for a downcast woman, but your presence and encouragement may be all she needs to get her through a difficult time.

title: Blessed Assurance
verse: "And so, after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise." (Hebrews 6:15)
content: It is recounted in this chapter the promise God made to Abraham and in this verse how he waited for its fulfillment and received it. The patient endurance of trusting the promises of God is not always easy. Abraham and in essence Sarah received a promise from God, but they did not receive this promise immediately. In fact it was more than ten years later before the promised came as Isaac.  If God has given you a promise, lay hold of it, it will happen.  There is nothing like a promise; explanations will not suffice and they change depending on the circumstance; only God’s promise will keep hope alive. It is His blessed assurance!

title: Led by the Light
verse: (Romans 8:14)“For those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God.”
content: Paul’s writing to the Romans about life through the Spirit.I often ask the question: “How do people survive without God in this world?”  It is a mystery and is something that cannot be done.The Holy Spirit’s presence is what helps the believer to make it through every day. Without being led by the Spirit our lives would be a mess.  Many times it is that still small voice that keeps us out of danger and gives us the solutions we need.Thank God for the precious Holy Spirit, who teaches, comforts, recalls things we have learned and counsels us.  Allow the Holy Spirit to help you.

title: Good over Evil
verse: “[Let] love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.” - (Romans 12:9)
content:
An imploration for Christians to live transformed lives, avoiding evil but doing good.

There are no two ways about it, good will triumph over evil.

Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn says this: The battle line between good and evil runs through the heart of every man.

As Christians we will continue to detest evil and embrace goodness for it is love, the love of Christ who first loved us that propels us to do that which is good.

Good over evil is standing up for the downtrodden, seeking the lost at all cost, caring for the desolate, feeding the hungry, praying for the troops, or paying a utility bill.

title: You will be Blessed
verse: (Luke 14:14)“…and you will be blessed. Although they cannot repay you, you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.”
content: Jesus tells the parable of the great supper.The parable of the great supper is a “simple” story that Jesus used to illustrate a spiritual lesson.  He encourages the invitation of the poor, crippled, lame and blind above friends, relatives or brothers to a dinner.  The rationale is that those types of people will not be able to repay you; your reward will come however in due time.  You will be blessed by God for your acts of kindness to those less fortunate.Endeavor to make a difference by extending kindness and courtesies; it is the way to live and honor God.

title: Compassion
verse:
“But whoso hath this world's good, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels [of compassion] from him, how dwelleth the love of 
God in him?” - (1 John 3:17)
content:
John commands the children of God to love each other.

The love of God dwelleth in you and me by compassion; it spurs us to respond to the needs of others.    

Caring for
Others
More, 
Pursuing
Another’s
Sake &
Situation 
Instead of 
Neglect

Compassion is a call to care about what others are going through and not to turn our backs, or a blind eye.

You and I have been recipients of compassion, if it were mot so we would not be where we are today. As we have been helped let us help others too.

title: Reward
verse: "Honor your father and your mother, as the LORD your God has commanded you, so that you may live long and that it may go well with you in the land the LORD your God is giving you.” - (Deuteronomy 5:16)
content:
This is listed as the fifth commandment that was given to the Israelites in the dessert; it is however the first command that has a promise. It has a promise of long life and happiness. 

It has no age limit and does not only apply to children, as adults we too should obey.

Beginning this New Year with a promise of living to an old age, with happiness, is more than one could ask for.  But it is in obeying this commandment, that we reap the reward.

title: In the Last Days
verse: (Acts 2:17) “In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people.  Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, your old men will dream dreams.”
content: Peter addresses the crowd and refers to the prophecy of Joel.We’ve seen much and have had countless experiences throughout our lifetime. Sometimes we are eager for more and other times we desire a reprieve.  For God time is eternity and what we experience is but minuscule in the scheme of things. God spoke through the prophet Joel about things yet to come – an awesome outpouring and empowerment of the Holy Spirit. In the last days the Spirit of God will reign supremely on all, wait patiently for it.

title: Where is God?
verse: "I have set the LORD always before me: because [he is] at my right hand, I will not be moved.”(Psalms 16:8)
content: David declares that he put the Lord in front of him always. Where are you in relation to God? Do you think of Him when you are about to choose an outfit? Or when you choose education, either for yourself or your children? Is He the first person you strive to please with your employment? Because David knew that God was always near to him, he did not worry about being defeated.  He was safe as long as the Lord was at the forefront of his life.Turn away from all the things that seek to steal that position that only God should have.

title: Do not Presume
verse: “Through presumption comes nothing but strife, but with those who receive counsel is wisdom.” - (Proverbs 13:10)
content:
A warning regarding presumptuousness.

Look at her, showing off, like she is all that, these were the thoughts that filled Teisha’s mind as she watched Marva. 

Teisha gossiped with other women about Marva and caused quite a stir.  

One day when Teisha was going through a tremendous challenge it was Marva who came to her aid.  Marva listened to Teisha as she talked, consoling and comforting her, after which she conveyed her life story to her.  

Teisha’s eyes popped at the wisdom she garnered from Marva and at the events of her life, oh how wrong she had been about her.  

“Do not presume” has become her mantra, it should be ours too.

title: The Son Who Saves
verse: (Matthew 1:21)“She will give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
content: The birth of Jesus is foretold by Matthew.Mary gave birth to a son, He is atypical; He is the Son who saves! We all need a savior; life is what it is and sometimes it’s too much for any one person to bear.  Here we have the assurance that Jesus, the son who saves, will save us from our sins.It’s reassuring to know that He also saves from danger, destruction, desolation and so much more.Let us therefore embrace Jesus the savior and be sure to introduce Him to others who are in need of a savior.

title: Established
verse: “Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.” - (Proverbs 15:22)
content:
Wise instructions to live by written in the Proverbs.

You have a decision to make; you’ve lost sleep and peace of mind agonizing over what’s to be done. You’ve kept it to yourself thinking that you can work it out on your own, but it’s bigger than you and your capabilities.  

You mention it reluctantly to a friend and before you know it you have a solution, you have help and you have insight. Wow! Why hadn’t I thought of that before?  

It was the insight of the opinion of others that established you, that gave you a firm footing making the decision easier, making it practically applicable for your life and circumstance.

title: The Foolishness of God
verse: “Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.” - (1 Corinthians 1:25)
content:
Paul writes this epistle because there were divisions in the church.

Foolishness and God should never be in the same sentence; they contradict each other.  God is NEVER foolish!  What we may perceive as weakness in Him is actually strength; we cannot fathom God, His ways are past finding out.

Let us pray: Father in heaven we thank you that we can trust you to work all things out for our good, especially those that seem foolish to us.  We praise you that your perceived weakness is indeed strength, you are assiduously working on our behalf and we magnify your Holy Name. Amen.

title: A You A Daughter of Abraham?
verse: (Galatians 3:7)“Understand, then, that those who have faith are children of Abraham.”
content: Paul writes to the Galatians about justification by faith.Faith is confidence, trust, conviction, belief, hope, certainty, dependence, dedication, commitment……Do you have faith? In the midst of all that is happening around you; things seen or not seen, things heard or not heard, war, peace, strife…….. are you exercising your faith?Are you a daughter of Abraham?I encourage you today to take faith as a belt and put it on wherever you go and whatever you face; it will give you hope to make it through the situations of life.  It is your faith in God that will stand the test of time when everything else falls.

title: Divinely Feminine
verse: " 'Haven't you read,' he replied, 'that at the beginning the Creator "made them male and female," and said, "For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh"? So they are no longer two, but one. Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate.' “ (Matthew 19:4-6)
content: Creation indicates that gender is a divine ordinance made by God, not a mistake in human development. In God’s picture of marriage, two persons who are divided by vast differences become one fruitful, complementary image. How is the unity in your marriage? As you conform to God’s role for you as a woman, you will find it easier to complement and unite with your husband.

title: Father
verse: “The Lord is like a father to his children, tender and compassionate to those who fear him.” (Psalm 103:13)
content: A song of praise for the Lord’s mercies.Father, Dad, Daddy, Pop, Poppa, Papa, Papi, Baba, Abba......Like a Father to us, tender and compassionate is the Lord.  I had a Dad who was negative, but when I came in contact with God the Father there was a remarkable difference.  I no longer cower in fear or cover my ears to block out the negativity.  This Father loves me! He loves me regardless and in spite of myself and guess what? He LOVES YOU TOO!Regardless of the type of relationship you had or didn’t have with your earthly Father, this Father is different.  He will not let you down!

title: The Spirit of the Lord
verse: “And the spirit of the LORD shall rest upon him, the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and of the fear of the LORD;” - (Isaiah 11:2)
content:
Isaiah’s writing about the righteous reign of the Messiah.

The Spirit of the Lord is an asset for our lives as believers; it is a vital necessity for survival, allowing us to partake of wisdom, understanding, counsel, might, knowledge and the fear of the Lord.

Although this passage refers to Christ the Messiah, we who are born again, have the same Spirit that Christ has.

May we continually allow the Spirit of the Lord to rest upon us, as it did on the Messiah and empower us throughout life’s journey.

title: Holding Back
verse: “A fool always loses his temper, but a wise man holds it back.” - (Proverbs 29:11)
content:
A word of wisdom from the Proverbs, this chapter is captioned: Happy is he who keeps the law.

It seemed like the perfect time for Alison to vent her feelings; she had been hurt, abused and misused.  It was time to set things right!  Before she could speak she heard a whisper saying, “I will take care of you, be quiet.” She was startled but she knew the prompting of the Holy Spirit all too well.  She then walked away as the tears streamed down her face.

Resolutions came shortly after in manner that astonished her, she was happy for holding back and being wise.  

Don’t lose your temper and be a fool!

title: Growing Up
verse: “Knowing that the testing of your faith produces patience.”  (James 1:3)
content: James writes about how we can profit from trials.Patience is the measure of how much we can endure, and how firm our convictions are. The trials of our faith are critical for revealing the true nature and maturity of our faith in Christ. Trials are not only revealing; they push us to grow. It is similarly like getting some lab work done for your doctor and the test not only reveals the cause but also directs to the cure of the aliment! Consider the daily trials of your life—how are you changing because of them? Are they making you more patient and revealing maturity, or are you caving under the pressure and acting immaturely?

title: Strong & Courageous
verse: “Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God [is] with thee whithersoever thou goest.” - (Joshua 1:9)
content:
After Moses’ death, the Lord gives Joshua a command that takes him through battles unto victory and hope.

The tapestry of our lives is woven from the many paths that we have trod. As we look back and move our hands over the textile we stop at various intervals reminiscing how a word from God gave us the strength and courage to make it through.

Strength and courage, YES, fear and dismay, NO, God is with you every step of the way.  It’s a command He gives, He is faithful!

title: Not yours to have
verse: “You shall not steal.”  (Exodus 20:15)
content: This is one of the ten commandants given to us to live by; the eight commandment.Stealing is not just taking something that doesn’t belong to you; it is also taking something that wasn’t meant for you. Whether it’s a charity, a discount, a refund, or any other system designed to help those in need, if you are taking it under false pretense, you are stealing. But there are also non-material things we take that are not meant for us; another person’s attention, a relationship, or virtue can be stolen by our selfish desires. Consider areas in your life where you might be gaining things that are not yours to have; reconcile them and live by this command.

title: Cast your Anxiety on Him
verse: (1 Peter 5:7)“Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.”
content: Peter’s final greetings and instructions in his first book.Unexpectedly you feel out of place, something is wrong. Your mind is racing and so is your hear, you are trying to hold on but everything seems to be eluding you.  Your body is tense, your heart reached to your mouth and you are certain that death is inevitable.  That’s the description an anxiety attack and for those of us who have had them it’s frightening and debilitating.The recommendation is to cast your anxiety on Him – Jesus Christ, and rid yourself of these attacks.  You will be able to breathe freely, experience peace and find solutions.  Go ahead and do it today!

title: One For All
verse: “For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes: first to the Jew, then to the Gentile.” - (Romans 1:16)
content:
This verse is a brief summary of the Christian faith by Paul. He preached the gospel of Christ with confidence in the message, but not with an ounce in his abilities and intellect (himself).

If you believe the gospel of Christ, it assures you of salvation regardless of your, gender, race or color.  Like a child who has total confidence in his parents’ ability to provide for him, so too we can be confident of salvation. Although it was first presented to the Jews, Gentiles are included; one plan for all people.

title: Blessing
verse: “Not rendering evil for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a blessing.” - (1 Peter 3:9)
content:
Peter intertwines how we are to behave with what we are to believe; practice and doctrine.

Inheriting a blessing is a good thing.

It is not wise to play tit for tat with your family, friends, co-workers, neighbors or anyone else when it comes to evil, contentions and such like. Be the woman who turns the other cheek and looks in the opposite direction.  It results in harmonious relations.  

As you replace good with evil, the lives that you’ll impact will be far reaching, so do what you have been called to do and you will inherit a blessing.

title: More than Silver and Gold
verse: “How much better to get wisdom than gold, to choose understanding rather than silver!”  - (Proverbs 16:16)
content:
Wisdom is better than Gold Solomon declares.

Here are some interesting facts about gold and silver.

Gold: 
•	Seen as a symbol of wealth.
•	Popular choice for achievement medals, statues and trophies.
•	Most popular precious metal for investment.

Silver: 
•	In approximately fourteen languages the words for money and silver are the same.
•	It is exceptionally shiny and the most reflective element.
•	A precious metal used to make jewelry, utensils and coins.

Regardless of those facts, wisdom and understanding are worth more than silver and gold.

King Solomon was the wisest man; his wisdom and words are credible so believe what he says, get wisdom and choose understanding over these precious metals.

title: Appear
verse: “When Christ, [who is] our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.” - (Collosians 3:4)
content:
Christ not only gives us life, He is our life. - Paul

From the Nile Valley in North Africa, Sudan and South Asia’s Pakistan, women face death everyday for their faith and believe in Jesus Christ.  For most of us it’s not that extreme, but dying to our fleshy desires is vital so that Christ can live in and through us.

Christ died for us, His life is manifested in our bodies; He lives through us. So in other words when He appears at the second coming, we too will appear being glorified with Him.

For Christ to have full reign in our lives, dying is recommended.

title: Things Above
verse: “Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.” - (Colossians 3:2)
content:
Paul urges the Colossians to live holy with eternity in view.

As a mother potty trains a child so too we must train our minds to think of heavenly things.  It is easy and effortless to set our minds on the things that we can see and understand rather than on things we can’t see nor understand.

It is to our benefit however, not to dwell on earthly things that are temporal but on things above that are eternal. This is also helpful to get us through the pitfalls of life, cushioning the effects, making them bearable.

When the dust is settled, the race is over; the curtain is drawn; only eternity will matter.

title: Biblical Neuroscience
verse: “And take the helmet of salvation,…”  (Ephesians 6: 17)
content: A helmet is used to demonstrate the place of salvation in our lives. Salvation is a protective cover. What was the purpose of the helmet as used in armor? It disguised the identity of the wearer. Likewise, salvation hides our identity in Christ, making our former self unrecognizable to the rest of the world. A helmet also filtered what affects the brain. It helped to ensure that the wearer did not sustain a brain injury during combat. Likewise, salvation should be a filter for what goes in and out of our minds. The purpose of the helmet of salvation is to keep us in the right frame of mind, by shaping the worldview used to evaluate God and others.

title: Be Still, He is God
verse: (Psalm 46:10a)“Be still, and know that I am God ” .
content: God is the refuge of His people who conquers nations.I don’t know about you but being still has never been one of my finer traits.  I fidget, squirm, complain and become frustrated by it.  Yet that is not an appropriate behavior for a child of God I know; but it still happens to me occasionally.Have you been there?Today’s word however gives an uncanny assurance and command: be still, He is God.It is enough to literally take Him at His word, He does not lie, what He says is true.  He is God who sits on the throne, rules supremely and has EVERYTHING under control; be still, He IS GOD!

title: Which Measure?
verse: (Mark 4:24b)"With the measure you use, it will be measured to you and even more."
content: Jesus speaks about one of the parables of the Kingdom.There is no way you can weight a pound of peas and end up with half a pound instead, expect that the scale is defective.  What is weighed will be the weight that is shown.How do we measure our lives and the things we do? Do we use full or half measure?  Do we do what we know to be true and upright with all our heart, mind and body or are we skimpy?Today let’s look at which measure we utilize because assuredly that’s what we will get in return. Ensure that it is the best measure.

title: Training
verse: “Train a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not turn from it.” - (Proverbs 22:6)
content:
This is a very wise saying from the book of wisdom.

We have all been trained in some way and can identify with the benefits that training gives.  As women we have the gift of nurturing even if we are not biological mothers.  We understand all too well the importance of training.  

Training produces a desired result; it seldom strays from the path it has embarked upon. We are living proof.  When we think back over our lives we can’t but follow the path that has been marked for us long ago.

Therefore be diligent to train knowing that the course will be maintained.

title: Keys of the Kingdom
verse: (Matthew 16:19)   “I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven; whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
content: Peter confesses Jesus as Christ.A key opens that which is locked, it gives you access whenever you want.  Just like the keys to your home or car which portrays ownership and gives free access, Jesus gives us the keys of the Kingdom which affords us free access.  It was upon Peter’s revelation and confession of who Jesus is that access is given. May we also come to this revelation especially throughout this season when He symbolically came into the world.Receive the keys of the Kingdom from Him today.

title: Take Heed
verse: “And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and [so] that day come upon you unawares.” - (Luke 21:34)
content:
Jesus talks about signs of the end of the age and implores us to be careful.

Pay attention and listen up! 

The end is near; things are winding down but be careful that your hearts do not get overloaded with consuming too much, with drunkenness and the cares of this life, so that you miss the day when Christ returns.

Father, in the name of Jesus, keep our hearts with all diligence, focused on you and not on the cares of this life. We put our trust in you, taking heed and leaning upon your everlasting arms. Amen.

title: Grace
verse: “Grace [be] with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.” - (Ephesians 6:24)
content:
Paul’s letter to the Ephesians is a revelation and reflection of God’s grace that he experienced.

My mother is always showing off her precious, beautiful vase. “Don’t touch it”, she always says.

It was so beautiful; I just wanted to feel it and see it up close.  When I came to myself, the vase was splinted on the ground.  I thought about where I could hide it.  I was terrified.

There was nowhere to hide it, when she got home I cowardly took the broken pieces to her. To my surprise she hugged me and said, “Its okay.”

Grace applied to me, to you, and to them that love the Lord. G-R-A-C-E.

title: Control
verse: “Sensible people control their temper; they earn respect by overlooking wrongs.” - (Proverbs 19:11)
content:
The fear of the Lord leads to life. 

Our society is fast pace driven and demands instantaneous responses most of the times and if we are not vigilant we fall prey to its dictates. 

Control is seemingly a “lost art” in the world today but is something that believers should practice in becoming more like Christ.  

As sober, sensible people we need to harness control in our dealings with others.

Let them refer to us as fools for overlooking wrongs, it is Christ that we emulate and to Him we ultimately answer.

Whether we know it or not, our actions are a witness tool for winning others to Christ who overlooked our faults when we accepted Him.

title: Answer to Anxiety
verse: “I prayed to the Lord, and he answered me. He freed me from all my fears.” (Psalm 34:4)
content: The exhaustive list of recognized anxiety disorders is a poignant expression of how many different ways people try to cope with fear, and fail miserably at it. Anxiety is caused when people try to cope with the lack of control over circumstances which stems from sins inflicted by others, by their own doing, and or as a result of medical issues. God expects us to surrender all of our fears to Him and trust Him to work for our good in every situation.  This requires living a life of total surrender; obedience to His Word and making lifestyle changes in order to become healthy in body and mind.

title: He has the Authority to Forgive
verse: (Luke 5:24)“But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.” So he said to the paralyzed man, “I tell you, get up, take your mat andgo home.”
content: Luke recounts Jesus healing a paralytic and his words about forgiveness.Johnny and his sister Sally visit their grandparents.  He accidentally kills his grandmother’s pet duck with his slingshot and buried it in the woods. His sister saw and blackmailed him into doing her chores; he got frustrated after awhile and mustered the courage to tell his grandmother what happened.  She told him she had seen it too, she loves and forgave him.Jesus, He has the authority to forgive, allow Him to do so.

title: Prisoners of Sin
verse: "But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe." (Galatians 3:22)
content: This verse starts by saying that there are no grounds for an argument about who is a sinner, or about how much power sin has over anyone. All people are prisoners of sin. Why does the Bible make this point so profoundly? For this purpose--that God's promise of salvation might be seen and understood to them that believe. Are you helping to spread this message that all are sinners, unable to overcome sin on their own, and are in need of a Savior? Do you tell the world where to find the Savior who sets prisoners of sin free?

title: Secret Place
verse: “He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.” - (Psalm 91:1)
content:
A safe abode when dwelling in the Presence of God. 

The secret place is the presence of God, dwelling there provides a refuge from difficulties, the shadow of being under His wings gives protection from the scorching sun. It’s a quiet, safe resting place.

It’s not easy to stay in God’s presence, Joan writes in her journal.  She knows the benefits and fights to stay there where she can better cope with life and its trials.  Being away from your presence has its consequences, she continues.

Oh Lord God, I pray that your presence would be of utmost importance for women everywhere because there you make us complete.

title: He's with Us!
verse: (Matthew 18:20)“For where two or three have gathered together in My name, I am there in their midst.”
content: The assurance of Jesus’ presence when we gather to pray.What a blessed assurance we have had over this holiday season to the testimony that Jesus Christ is with us every step and every day of our lives; through good and bad days and circumstances.He’s with us no matter what happens! And even more so when we gather to pray, His presence is there in the midst of us.Have you ever been to a women’s prayer meeting and felt the presence of God in a mighty way? That is the fulfillment of this passage, therefore be bold and confident in prayer when gathered together in His name.

title: Commit your Children
verse: “By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.”   (Hebrews 11:23)
content: Moses’ mother lived through an ordeal no mother wants to. If she, a godly woman had confessed to giving birth to a son, he would have been killed by the authorities. She took a huge step of faith in God by hiding him. As Christian mothers, we will encounter circumstances where we must decide if we are going to give our children to the world and it’s systems to kill them spiritually, or are we going to shelter them for God and His purposes. Commit your children to the Lord and trust Him.

title: Convicted Consumers
verse: “You shall not covet…”  (Exodus 20:17)
content: Global trade encourages women to consume more in hopes of having a fulfilled life. Covetousness is hard to overcome and even more so with the media depicting that our dreams can be fulfilled through the acquisition of “new” things.Covetousness has become a lifestyle in many places around the world. We can avoid this temptation by discontinuing email and catalogue subscriptions, but a change in behavior alone will not change our hearts. Hebrews 13:5 says, “Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as you have: for he has said, I will never leave you, nor forsake you.” By accepting Christ’s promise and presence in our lives, He fills every void and we can overcome covetousness.

title: Ministering to Jesus
verse: “There were also many women there, looking on from a distance who had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to him.” - (Matthew 27:55)
content:
Matthew’s record of the events that occurred after Jesus’ death.

A movement had begun, it was in high gear, it seemed like it would last forever and then the leader was taken into custody, tried and put to death.  

Hopes and dreams shattered to the ground, what will become of the movement, who will lead them? 

These women were now looking from a distance after Jesus was crucified.  Ministering to Jesus was a joy for them, now, nothing.  But that was before they experienced the resurrected Christ.

Continue ministering to the body of Christ regardless of shattered dreams and hopes, resurrection is coming!

title: The Weapon of our Warfare
verse: “…and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.” (Ephesians 6:17)
content: The sword is the first point of contact with the enemy.  It pierces through the opponent’s armor straight to the heart. It intimidates the opponents; brings them to their knees in surrender, to death, or to silent retreat. Likewise, the Sword of the Spirit, the Word of God is our first point of contact with the lost world; it is our witness. It divides thoughts and intents such as inconsistencies in worldviews. It pierces through the hardest heart and causes it to come to terms with spiritual issues left unattended. There are three responses to the Word; 1) surrender, 2) defeat, 3) retreat.  It is the weapon of our warfare!

title: The Gift
verse: (2 Corinthians 9:15)Thanks be to God for His indescribable gift!
content: Paul encourages the Corinthians to give to the needy.It’s that time of year when gift giving is at its optimal peak. Do you love receiving gifts?Over the Christmas season countless gifts will be bought, given and received; gifts of varying types, sizes and cost.The gift spoken of here however is an indiscernible gift; words pale and fail to describe how precious it is and what its value is.This gift keeps on blessing because that’s the nature of it and it can do nothing less.  Like God let us endeavor to give our best gift all the time to others because God gave his best gift to us and especially throughout this Christmas season.

title: Pure Religion
verse: “Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this, to visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, [and] to keep himself unspotted from the world.” - (James 1:27)
content:
James’ writing to the twelve tribes to be doers and not hearers only of the word of God.

Nicole is a volunteer at the shelter that helps orphans, widows, and drug addicts.  That’s what they call her there but to her it’s more than that. When she became a Christian she decided that she would do everything possible in her will to help those in affliction.  

She was once a product of that kind, so she fully understands and is aware of the things such a person goes through. 

Unawares, she is practicing pure religion and keeping pure.

title: Be Watchful
verse: “Jesus answered: “Watch out that no one deceives you. For many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am the Messiah,’ and will deceive many.” - (Matthew 24:4-5)
content:
On the Mount of Olives, Jesus answers his disciples regarding the signs of His coming.

Jesus is the real deal, He’s not a counterfeit.

In order to identify counterfeit money you need to handle the real money, get to know its features and how it feels.

To know Jesus we must spend time with Him, get to know his characteristics and read His word to know how He operates and what He would say.  

Imitators will arise claiming to be the Messiah so we need to be watchful.  Our personal relationship with Him will help us to avoid being misled.

title: Togetherness
verse: “And let us consider how we may spur one another on toward love and good deeds.  Let us not give up meeting together, as some are in the habit of doing, but let us encourage one another--and all the more as you see the Day approaching.” - (Hebrews 10:24-25)
content:
Hebrews is a commentary on the Old Testament, explaining how Jesus brought a better covenant to replace the Jewish laws and customs.

Cheering each other on is a vital part of surviving in this world, so too is fellowship (meeting together).

None of us can withstand the pressures of life by ourselves; with others (one or more),you can soar to new heights and achieve many goals.

Togetherness works, try it for yourself and see.

title: The Good News about Bad News
verse: “He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord.”  (Psalm 112:7)
content: Christians should not be overcome by “bad news”, but it does not mean that they are faithless if it oppresses them. Bad news pushes us to ask difficult questions about God and our faith. No matter how bad the news is, although we may be tempted like Job’s wife to disassociate ourselves from God, He wants us to respond in humility and faith, accepting His will and trusting the plan He has for us. Bad news doesn’t mean God has forsaken or cursed us; on the contrary, it means God has specifically chosen a trial that will bring Him glory and allow us through faith, to grow.

title: All Times
verse: “A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.” - (Proverbs 17:17)
content:
This proverb compares a friend with a brother, showing that a friend loves freely by choice, but a brother loves by the obligation of relation. 

Family members will stick by you when you are going through a rough patch.  You know they love you, they are family and they rise to the occasion in times of distress.

Your girlfriend however is there for you all the time, up into your face and into your business; she knows the flavor of your “Kool-Aid.” 

She loves you when whether you are stupid, funny, lazy, angry, emotional, broke, single, or crazy, she just loves you!

Embrace and enjoy the benefits of friendship and family.

title: A Holy Name
verse: “You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain; for the Lord will not hold him unpunished who takes His name in vain.”  (Exodus 20:7)
content: The way that we use or say a name says a lot about how we feel about a person. For instance, when women value a relationship with an individual they often use pet names. However, when the relationship is strained, they tend to revert to a first name or title basis with the individual. Therefore, when and how it is used speaks to the true nature of the relationship. Likewise, if we use God’s name dishonorably instead of speaking to or about Him directly, it is an indication that the relationship is strained. His Holy name requires reverence.

title: Secret of Relationships
verse: “Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.”  (1 Peter 2:7)
content: All relationships should be marked with honor. Remember that all people are made in the image of God and what you do and say to them, you do and say to Christ.There should be love for the brotherhood, the church. Our relationship with the church should be marked by sacrifice and service, not just by attendance. Fear God: In His Word, the fear of the Lord is to depart from iniquity; those who truly respect the Almighty will depart from sin.Honor the King: God expects us to obey, respect and serve those who govern our country, whether they do it with righteousness or wickedness. These are the secrets of a relationship.

title: Call on the Name of the Lord
verse: (Acts 2:21)“And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.”
content: A portion of Peter’s sermon to the people during Pentecost referenced from what Joel the prophet had spoken. In times of deep distress and sorrow sometimes it’s only the whisper of the name of the Lord that gets one through turmoil.  Even as the tears stream down a face or runs silently inside with each breath you wait for salvation to come.Calling on the name of the Lord is therapeutic; it calms your nerves, soothes your doubts, strengthens your bones, lifts your spirit, and definitely saves you.Whatever the situation you are facing today, call on the name of the Lord, you will be saved!

title: Bind and Loose
verse: (Matthew 18:18)“Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
content: Jesus tells what will happen when the power of binding and loosing is employed in prayer.Simply put to bind and loose is to prevent and sanction by an unquestionable authority.  As Christians we have that authority so we are more than eligible to do both.Placing handcuffs, much like rope or chain in the physical is binding and loosing is removing those restraints. I am sure you know of situations that need to be bound and others that need to be loosed, therefore bind and loose with the authority that’s been given to you.

title: Be Content
verse: “Then some soldiers asked him, "And what should we do?" He replied, "Don't extort money and don't accuse people falsely--be content with your pay." - (Luke 3:14)
content:
John the Baptist prepares the way for Jesus.

Each morning we get a new chance at life, a clean slate to start over again; utilize it wisely.

In this passage John answers the questions of those who came to be baptized.  He told the soldiers to be content with their wages, don’t extort or accuse others wrongly.  Likewise, we should be content with our wages and avoid extortion and false accusation of others.

It’s a code of conduct to live by; it honors God and lays a foundation for Christian witness.    

But godliness with contentment is great gain. (1 Timothy 6:6)

title: God Answers
verse: “When I called, you answered me; you made me bold and stouthearted.” (Psalm 138:3)
content: This Psalm declares the Lord’s goodness to the faithful.Megan had so many questions: “Why did my parents die?” “What is the purpose of my life?” “When will the struggles end?”  “Who is responsible for this mess?”“Where will help come from?”Like Megan we all have questions that we need answers for and it’s only God who will give us answers that will suffice.God answers questions.  Wherever you are and whatever situation you find yourself in today call out to Him. He’ll make you bold, courageous and steadfast as you go through the obstacles and turns of life. He’ll cause you to triumph.  Call to Him today and every day, He’ll answer.

title: Love Does Compassionate Things
verse: “But when he saw the multitude, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.”(Matthew 9:36)
content: People around the world hunger and thirst for Godly leadership, they are as sheep without a shepherd yearning for someone to point them in the right direction.   Women should support strong Godly men, to help them become the leaders for those who are seeking spiritual knowledge. We must first have a heart of compassion for the multitudes. Compassion propels us to make significant sacrifices, to see others saved and led by God, through the leadership of these Godly men. We should also strive for dependence on God and in so doing; we will understand how much others need Him too.

title: Ask, Seek, Knock
verse: “Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:” - (Matthew 7:7)
content:
Jesus tells us to ask, seek and knock for what we desire.

A child is relentless and fearless in pursuit of what he or she wants. They will ask for what they want without processing the claim beforehand.  They ask!

Here Jesus tells us to ask, seek and knock for the things we desire.  It’s common sense really, but our adult minds sometimes rationalize everything to the nth degree and before we know it, we chicken out and keep silent.

Today, we will let common sense prevail; we will ask to get, seek to find, and knock for the door to be opened for us.

title: Tangible Faith
verse: “Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.”  (Hebrews 11:1)
content: A woman’s life is a walk of faith. We must walk by faith in God, and we must also walk by faith with the head of our home. By faith we trust that the head of our home has our best interest at heart, that he will provide for us, and that he will make decisions that are God-honoring. There are times when he will make a poor decision that will affect the wellbeing of the family. What do we do in these circumstances? We must have faith that God will take care of us and our families and favor us in the circumstance. Look with expectancy, God WILL be faithful!

title: My Shepherd
verse: “The LORD [is] my shepherd; I shall not want.” - (Psalms 23:1)
content:
The most famous Psalm: The Lord, the Shepherd of His People.

We are likened unto sheep in this passage and The Lord as our Shepherd.

Sheep are directionless, weak, dumb, dependent and defenseless.  Without the guidance of a shepherd they will be vulnerable and lost.  

The Lord’s my shepherd; I shall not want, we are unable to provide for ourselves so the Lord, the good shepherd that He is, makes the necessary provision that we need for every day and circumstance of our lives. 

You are in need of a shepherd, is He your shepherd? He is willing and capable so entrust your life to Him. He will take care of you!

title: Words
verse: “May these words of my mouth and this meditation of my heart be pleasing in your sight, Lord, my Rock and my Redeemer.” - (Psalm 19:14)
content:
This passage is taken from one of the many Psalms written by David.
Like a stack of cards we never know which hand we’ll be dealt, until we lay hold of it, so too in life, we are dealt many different “hands”, but God is our Rock and our Redeemer.  So whatever comes our way in life, we can still speak words of praise and our hearts can magnify God regardless of our circumstances. 

As we look beyond our present condition, we have hope in the Lord, in His promises and our words can be pleasing to God, who is our refuge.

title: Healing
verse: “Reckless words pierce like a sword, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.” - (Proverbs 12:18)
content:
Solomon writes about the tongue, healthy and unhealthy speech.

Whenever I am going through a difficult time, especially if it stems from my folly, I do not want nor need to hear that it’s my fault.  What I need is a listening ear and words of encouragement to get me through.  I already know that I did wrong.  Can you relate?

We must endeavor to use our tongue to edify, bring healing instead of tear down and yield wounding. 

The tongue of the wise brings health, the process of making or becoming sound or healthy again.  There is no honor in being bitter and sharp tongued. Our words should bring healing all the time.

title: Little is Much
verse: “Truly I tell you, anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name because you belong to the Messiah will certainly not lose their reward.”  (Mark 9:41)
content: Christians do not give glory to God through praises only, but also by doing good works. Housewives especially tend to feel that they are doing nothing for the Gospel’s sake; that they are isolated from the world, and they are insignificant to God. Not so. Every choice you make to do the right thing, even though small, such as giving a cup of water has its reward. Women, take comfort in how valuable you are to God. You are laboring daily for the Gospel through patience and diligence, showing love to the Lord and your family.

title: Encourager
verse: “We faced conflict from every direction, with battles on the outside and fear on the inside.  But God, who encourages those who are discouraged, encouraged us by the arrival of Titus. His presence was a joy.”  (2 Corinthians 7:5-6)
content: Paul writes about joy in the midst of tribulation to the Corinthians.  Life can be a battle; many things come at us from different angles constantly.  Things are happening around and within us, causing us to fear and to dread, but God is our encourager in the midst of it all.Sometimes He does so Himself and other times He sends someone like Titus, the encourager.Jane needs to hear that she will still make it despite her failures. We are benefactors of encouragement, so please, encourage the “Jane” in your life.

title: Do you love Him?
verse: “He who has My commandments and keeps them is the one who loves me;” (John 14:21)
content: Every mother knows how foolish and hurtful it is for a child to use confessions of love to escape doing what they are told. When a rebellious child professes to love his mother, he communicates a message of disregard and apathy towards her by disobedience. This is not love. Likewise, when we refuse to obey God and look for opportunities to serve Him in self-gratifying ways we are like that child who says “I love you” but shows disregard and apathy toward God and His Word. The test of our love is this: if we love Him, we will obey Him and keep His commandments. Do you love Him?

title: Your Soul
verse: “For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?” - (Matthew 16:26)
content:
Peter’s confession of his faith in Christ.

What is your soul worth? 
Can its worth be measured materially? 
What would you exchange for your soul? 

Valuable, thought provoking questions, that only YOU can answer. 

Your soul is the spiritual part of you, your emotions and intellect, the part of you that will last for eternity.

I can assure you that NOTHING is more precious than your soul which is the sum total of who you are.

You will need your soul in the long run so give it to God, He’ll keep it safe.

title: Praise at the City Gate
verse:
“Charm is deceptive, and beauty is fleeting; but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.
Give her the reward she has earned, and let her works bring her praise at the city gate.” - (Proverbs 31:30-31)
content:
You’ve seen and admired women who have stood the test of time and wondered, “Can I be like her, do I have the courage?”  Women like Abigail in I Samuel 25, Eleanor Roosevelt and Rosa Parks.

You have got it in you, you love God, you keep His commands and strive to become more like Jesus.

It’s not how you look, it has nothing to do with your charisma, the fact that you fear God, you will be rewarded with praise at your city gate.

title: Busy Watching
verse: “She watches over the affairs of her household and does not eat the bread of idleness.” - (Proverbs 31:27)
content:
This is one of the traits listed in Proverbs31 of the Virtuous Woman.

Week after week, month after month, year after year some things don’t change in your life.  You are responsible for making sure the home is tidy, the groceries are bought, the laundry is done, the meals are cooked, car pooling is organized and the list could go on and on.  These activities are ongoing; you are busy watching over the affairs of your household.

Be assured that your reward is coming so continue to diligently perform your duties as you box away the bread of idleness. Your watching will bear fruit.

title: Come Worshipping
verse: "And a leper came to Him and bowed down before Him, and said, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean." (Matthew 8:2)
content: Prayer can be a difficult discipline to establish as part of your life, particularly when your needs are too demanding to put into words. What advice can we take from this leper about bringing our requests to God with worship? First, that worship is directed to God, he did not seek the help of anyone but God to meet his needs. Second, that he recognizes God's sovereignty in his request by saying "Lord" and asking for His will to be done. Third, he acknowledges God's power over his situation. Like this leper come worshipping Christ as you bring your requests.

title: Unique
verse: “You made all the delicate, inner parts of my body and knit me together in my mother's womb.” (NLT) - (Psalm 139:13)
content:
The Psalm of David, which explains God’s perfect knowledge of man.

From the embryonic stages God uniquely developed your inner parts: organs, cells, tissue; your DNA.  No one else comes close to your prototype and they never will. Think about it, the intricacies, and attributes, preferences that you have as a woman was intricately woven and orchestrated by God, making you unique.

Embrace and celebrate who you are because it is God who has fashioned you, and He knows everything about you.  Nothing is hidden from Him; He cares about you.  From the womb to the tomb He’ll be there for you.

title: Everlasting Life
verse: “For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.” - (John 3:16)
content:
The famous and profound passage about salvation through Jesus Christ, what He did for mankind.

If you were the only woman in the world, God would still send His Son Jesus to die for you.  That’s how much God loves you.

Life may or may not be good right now, but think of the prospect of living forever in a perfect, righteous and fair world. Jesus made it possible by shedding His blood on the cross just for you.

It’s not a fairytale but it has a happy ending, the promise of eternal life when you accept Jesus Christ.

title: Edification
verse: “Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.” - (Romans 14:19)
content:
The laws of love and liberty expressed to the Romans and to us through Paul.

Sharon and Betty have been friends for many years, they do not always agree but they strike a balance by making peaceful resolutions and endeavoring to build each other up.  This is no easy accomplishment; they both work towards it every day.

Their words and deeds work together; praying together, avoiding slander and gossip, forgiving quickly, and communicating effectively for mutual edification.

Peace is to be pursued; it makes for a happier, healthier, life and environment. 

Like Sharon and Betty, let us strive to maintain peace and to edify each other.

title: Many Bitter Troubles
verse: "You who have shown me many troubles and distresses will revive me again, and will bring me up again from the depths of the earth.” (Psalm 71:20)
content: The Psalmist declares that God is the rock of salvation.We often keep record of our lives based on the many bitter troubles we have endured: that was the summer I was injured, that was the year we lost a relative,... But the presence of God in the midst of each trouble should never be overlooked. There is not a single situation that He will not help us through. Instead of counting the many bitter troubles, acknowledge the presence and blessings of God in every situation. He is faithful and will revive and bring you through them all.

title: The Sovereign God
verse: (Jeremiah 32:27)“See, I am the Lord, the God of all flesh; is anything too hard for me?”
content: Jeremiah buys a field and after giving the title, this verse is part of his prayer. I am intrigued and comforted by Maurette Brown’s The Sovereign God lyrics which in part say:   “A wave of your hand can command the seas to hold their peace If you can handle the seas Then I know that you can deal with all my needsSo I put every situation into your capable handsHe is able to do exceedingly abundantly Above all we ask or thinkSo take all your burdens And lay them at His feet and watch Him meet the need”There is NOTHING too hard for God!

title: Peace in a Godly Leader
verse: "With him is an arm of flesh; but with us is the Lord our God to help us, and to fight our battles. And the people rested themselves upon the words of Hezekiah king of Judah." (2 Chronicles 32:8)
content: National peace is a gift of God and a blessing that can be lost by disobedience to God. In constitutional republics, such as the United States, and similar governments, women play an important role in deciding who their future leaders will be. By being politically active, our civic duty is to ensure that leaders who honor God with their whole heart come to power. We must chose people like Hezekiah whose words can be trusted and whose trust is in the Lord--not in their own power.

title: Hunger that will be Satisfied
verse: "Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.”  (Luke 6:35)
content: Have you ever had a craving for something, to the point that it almost caused you pain because you wanted it so badly? No matter what you tried to satisfy the craving, nothing else sufficed?God wants us to desire righteousness in this manner. We should be obsessed with doing the right thing for the sake of pleasing God. Nothing in the world should pacify the intensity of this craving. Has your desire for God ever slipped? Stimulate your desire for Him again with praise for His goodness during times of personal worship in His Word. He will fill you; He will fill your hunger and thirst.

title: Melody
verse: “Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;” - (Ephesians 5:19)
content:
A book written for the discouraged who are wondering if God really cares, it expresses the depth, breath, height and width of God’s love for the Christian.

They call you a mad woman, that’s alright.  You know what God has done in your life and in your family.  You walk around praising God like it’s nobody’s business.  Your mouth is moving and sounds are coming out, “Sshh, be quiet”, you are told.  But you know you can’t keep still because if it wasn’t for God you don’t know who or where you’d be.

You are an orchestra all by yourself making melody to God.

title: Wealth
verse: “Honor the LORD with your wealth, with the firstfruits of all your crops;” - (Proverbs 3:9)
content:
Guidance for the wise is aptly written in Proverbs.

Everything thing that you have; money or possessions was given to you by God.

He provided a job, gave you your skills, strength, health, gave you intellect and wisdom.  If it weren’t for Him you would have nothing.  It is a privilege therefore to honor, pay tribute to God with your wealth.

Wealth is a large quantity of money or possessions.  Here are some ways you can honor God with them:
•	Give money to charities and the poor.
•	Provide board and lodging for the less fortunate
•	Help someone get an education
•	Cook a meal for a neighbor

Honor the Lord with your wealth.

title: With all your Heart
verse: “Give me understanding and I will obey your instructions; I will put them into practice with all my heart.” - (Psalm 119:34)
content:
Meditations on the Excellencies of the Word of God from the longest chapter in the Bible.

The understanding that we need to obey God’s instructions, is of a spiritual nature; our carnal minds will not grasp God’s laws.  

Once we have gotten this divine understanding, our hearts will be set on doing the will of God, set on obeying his instructions.  

When we understand something, it results in acceptance of that thing because our minds can rationalize it. Understanding “why”, leads us to be obedient and it becomes easy to execute with all our heart.

Therefore get understanding to obey and follow with all your heart.

title: Every Tear
verse: “He will wipe every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death or mourning or crying or pain, for the old order of things has passed away." - (Revelation 21:4)
content:
John’s revelation of the New Jerusalem on the Isle of Patmos.

You’ve cried so much, you’ve seen your mother and grandmother cry; tears are everywhere.  Now here is this great promise, to wipe away EVERY tear.  Is it true?

Tears of grief breaks even the toughest person, it strikes a chord which we know quite well.  Sorrow, unhappiness, loss, hurt, pain, and heartbreak produces many tears, leaves us reeling, holding on for dear life though sometimes we would rather die to ease the sting.

The promise is real and true, He will wipe away EVERY tear from our eyes.

title: Love not the World
verse: “Love not the world, neither the things [that are] in the world. If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.” - (1 John 2:15)
content:
John beseeches that we do not love the world.

The world offers the good, the bad, and the indifferent.  The preferences we develop and the choices we make mirrors where our heart lies, for God or the world.

Achievements and temptations assail us in our daily lives and it is in the active pursuit of balance that we will be victorious in overcoming the world and demonstrating our love for God.

We will be known as Christian women who love not the world but who love the Father, because we pursue Godly things.

title: The Way, Truth and Life
verse: “Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.” - (John 14:6)
content:
Jesus is described as the Son of God by John and highlights His deity.

If you want to find the way to God, know the truth and get eternal life there is only ONE way, one avenue, and one person – Jesus Christ.

Anything or anyone else who offers such a package is false.  

To get to the President you must go through a security guard (several in fact), you will not get to him any other way.  Sure you can try, but it will be a useless effort.

Cut to the chase; go through Jesus, the way, truth and life.

title: Increase
verse: “And the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all [men], even as we [do] toward you:” - (Thessalonians 3:12)
content:
Paul records Timothy’s encouraging report.

“More, more, I want more,” shouted Jennifer above the other children.  She wanted more ice cream and she wasn’t afraid to say so.

Increase means to add more on what you already have.  Increasing in love towards each other is not like Jennifer asking for more ice cream or is it? 

Love for each other should increase and flourish more and more as we walk daily with the Lord. 

That’s what we should strive for above all else, shout for it like Jennifer by actively working at it, making it a verb and not just a noun.

title: Nesting in God
verse: "He will cover you with his feathers, and under his wings you may seek refuge: his truth is a shield and buckler.”  (Psalms 91:4)
content: God often uses words to form mental pictures to give us a clear understanding of His Word. In this verse, He relates to Himself as a bird: “feathers” and “wings” and to His truth as “shield” and “buckler.”     I have limited knowledge about shields and bucklers, but I have cared for ducks and chickens.  When a hen hatches her clutch she becomes attached and devoted to her chicks.      She protects her chicks under her wings; her strongest feathers make an armory around her chicks and the soft interior beneath her wings creates a safe place.      This picture reflects God’s protective love for us.

title: Fruit of the Spirit
verse: "But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith," - (Galatians 5:22)
content:
Paul writes to the Galatians about the works of the Spirit.

Like an orange with segments, so too is the fruit of the Spirit, notice it is not described as fruits of the Spirit.

Innately we are not born saints, but shaped in iniquity so it is natural and easy for us to react negatively rather than positively.

To develop the fruit of the Spirit we must be deliberate, every second, every minute, of every day until we exhibit love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith in response to every situation that we face.

It may take time, but commit to develop the fruit of the Spirit.

title: Except the Lord
verse: “Except the LORD build the house, they labour in vain that build it: except the LORD keep the city, the watchman waketh [but] in vain.” - (Psalms 127:1)
content:
The Psalmist explains working alongside the Lord, trusting and depending upon Him.

It is a futile, wasted, useless, vain, pointless, unsuccessful effort we make when we don’t acknowledge God and invite Him to help us with what we do. It will crumble, come to ruin, be invaded or demolished.  

Whatever it is that you are embarking upon, whether it is building a house or being a watchman, as mentioned in this passage, or raising children, building a career or marriage in this present age, God’s involvement guarantees success.

Invite Him to help; except the Lord is in it, it will come to naught.

title: Why this Weakness?
verse: “And on the basis of faith in His name, it is the name of Jesus which has strengthened this man whom you see and know; and the faith which comes through Him has given him his perfect health in the presence of you all.”  (Acts 3:16)
content: Have you ever wondered why a particularly difficult day or situation happened to you? Like this man in Acts, trials assail us allowing the world to see and know of our weaknesses. But the world, having seen our weakness, will be stunned by the strength of character God builds in us through them. With faith in Christ, the worst situations can be overcome, even as the whole world watches. When we are weak, Christ’s strength is manifested in great power.

title: With All
verse: “And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.” - (Deuteronomy 6:5)
content:
From the fifth book of Moses comes the great commandment, love the Lord your God.

All your heart, all your soul and all your might, with all that you have you are commanded to Love the Lord your God.

He created you, planned your life, has been with you, stood up for you, fought for you, died for you, opened doors for you, provided for you, kept you in your right mind, healed you from sickness, protected you; he’s done it ALL for you.

It’s easier when viewed like that and you will respond with your all; mind, soul and might.

title: Using Faith on Purpose
verse: “in addition to all, taking up the shield of faith with which you will be able to extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one.”  (Ephesians 6:16)
content: Paul uses a shield to relate for us what faith is and how we should use it. Faith is the shield we hold over our upper body to block doubts and sin that tempts us to live contrary to God’s Word. Faith must be used intentionally against the lies, tricks and oppression of the enemy to see the victories we desire. Although knowledge and righteousness are important, neither of these can take the place of faith as our defense. Faith should be our only defense that shields us from the devices of the enemy, therefore use faith on purpose.

title: A Cup of Water
verse: “For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.” - (Mark 9:41)
content:
Mark’s record of Jesus saying, “Whoever is not against us for us.”

Attributes of the Christian includes, hospitality, resourcefulness, humility, enthusiasm, generosity, flexibility and gratefulness.  

During the summer it’s so hot and humid, for the Christian who possesses these qualities it is easy to give a cup of water to quench someone’s thirst.  

Everything we do for Christ will be rewarded.  

A cup of water, a visit to the hospital, cooking a meal for the indigent, a visit to prison; these things are what we do in the name of Christ.

title: A Room For You
verse: “In my Father's house are many rooms; if it were not so, I would have told you. I am going there to prepare a place for you.” - (John 14:2)
content:
Jesus comforts His Disciples before His crucifixion. 

A promise of an eternal dwelling, what more can one ask for? 

A room for you and a room for me, this promise from Jesus is always viable and timely. It brings comfort to us when things are awry; sickness in the body, loss of job, or death in the family.  It gives us an eternal perspective and hope, to make it through.

Now and then: hardships now and glory then; present and future.

Momentary afflictions, but a room prepared in our Father’s house is worth enduring till then.

title: Saved
verse: “This [is] a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.” - (1 Tomothy 1:15)
content:
Fist and second Timothy were written in Paul’s old age to Timothy, a young man who was converted during one of Paul’s missionary journeys.

Looking back over your life you shake your head and say thank you God for saving a rebel such as I.  No matter your past, whether you struggled with addiction, loose and reckless living, or you thought you had it all together. You were a sinner like the rest of us and Christ came and saved you! 

He saved you from yourself; you know who you were, what you did and how you got by.  Thank God for His saving grace and salvation!

title: Open Arms
verse: “She opens her arms to the poor and extends her hands to the needy.” - (Proverbs 31:20)
content:
A characteristic trait of a virtuous woman.

I am sure you’ve heard of Mother Theresa. She helped the poor, helpless, sick and needy. She was awarded the 1979 Nobel Peace Prize and other awards including the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Ronald Reagan.  

All she did was open her arms and extended her hands.  You too can do the same in your sphere of influence.

How can you help the single mother across the street, the homeless man at the bus stop or the ragged looking child at the school your child attends? It does not matter how small an act it is, it will bring relief, so do it.

title: Good Counsel
verse: “The godly offer good counsel; they teach right from wrong.’’ - (Psalm 37:30)
content:
David gives a characteristic of the Godly.

Godly people on a whole speak positively and offer insight and intellect.

Our speech and instructions are important as children of light, as salt of the earth.  If we don’t give good counsel and teach what is right who will?  

I believe it is our responsibility to represent Christ in all that we do; countless people are influenced by our actions than what we are privy to.

We are the ones who bring life and hope into situations everywhere we go.  It’s much more than we know and it’s much bigger than we are.  We will open our mouths giving good counsel and teach right from wrong to a dying desperate world.

title: Party in Heaven
verse: “Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.” - (Luke 15:10)
content:
The parable of the lost coin.

There is a joy in Heaven when a sinner repents.

The streamers are in place, a few balloons here and there, colorful light, food, dancing and music!  Scenes of a party …… let your imagination run wild for a bit, can you see the angels at the party in Heaven?  

Every time someone comes receives Christ there is rejoicing, there is joy, and there is a party in Heaven.  

A party is the celebration of a special occasion.  Accepting Christ is the most important event in your life and it is worth celebrating every single time.

title: Repentance
verse: (Luke 5:31-32) “And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance”.
content: Luke writes about the calling of Levi also known as Matthew.Lucy has learned that repentance is not a word to dread but rather an experience to relish.  She will tell you that her life has seen many turns, stops and mishaps.  Some of which were created by her, while others were due to circumstances. Regardless of the origin, she learned to quickly walk through the process of repentance when necessary to regain peace and composure.None of us are born sinless; repentance is necessary in order to remain in right standing with the Father.

title: A Cheerful Giver
verse: “Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, [so let him give]; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.” - (2 Corinthians 9:7)
content:
Paul writes about the importance of giving cheerfully. 

Skipping happily along, Melanie held fifteen dollars tightly in her hands.  She was headed to the Red Cross office to make a donation for the flood victims she heard about on the news.  At ten years old she understood fully well the importance of giving. After all she had watched her mom giving for all her life. 

God loves a cheerful giver like Melanie, who decided that she would give it all for the cause. 

Have you decided what you will give? Be purposeful, willing and happy about it.

title: Legacy
verse: “Only be careful, and watch yourselves closely, so that you do not forget the things your eyes have seen or let them slip from your heart as long as you live. Teach them to your children and to their children after them.” - (Deuteronomy 4:9)
content:
The Israelites had been through the desert where they saw God perform astonishing miracles.  Here God reminds them to not forget all that He has done and in addition to teach this to their children.

It is very easy to forget the things God has done for us when the pressures of life hit. Therefore be mindful and deliberate to write them down so that you will remember and also leave a legacy for those who will read it.

title: Love, Love, Love
verse: “As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.” - (John 15:9)
content:
The basic facts about Jesus are written in the book of John with the emphasis here on relationship.

Your mother loves and cares for you, you love and care for your daughter, she loves and cares for your grandchild. 

Love begets love, love reciprocates love.

Jesus’ love for us naturally causes us to love others, His continuous love for us persuades us to continue the chain of love.

The love Jesus received from the Father, He deserved as the Son of God, He in turn loves us with the same kind of love and encourages us to continue with that kind of love.  Love, love, love.

title: Good or Bad?
verse: “For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things [done] in [his] body, according to that he hath done, whether [it be] good or bad.” - (2 Corinthians 5:10)
content:
Paul writes about the judgment seat of Christ.

All people including believers will stand before the judgment seat of Christ where our “works” in the flesh will be judged.   

Have we done good or bad “works” in the flesh?  
 
Our deeds, words, actions, and perceptions are considered works of the flesh. Good works are obeying God’s law and bad works are disobedience to His law.

It’s not about salvation and eternal life in this instance for the believer, but reward for doing what is good. For us, good works take precedence.

title: The Greatest of These
verse: “And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these [is] charity.” - (1 Corinthians 13:13)
content:
Paul states here that faith, hope and charity (love) will be with us forever, though eternity.

The love expressed in 1 Corinthians 13 is no mediocre kind of love, it is rooted and stems out of God’s love.

How does love then becomes the greatest of these? It achieves that status from the viewpoint that if we don’t have love, what we do will be shallow and abased. 

God’s love is spiritual, not carnal.  We will not be successful at expressing love except through the Spirit of God, trying to truly love in our flesh will not work.

Spiritual love is the greatest of these.

title: Do You Believe?
verse: (Matthew 21:22)"If you believe, you will receive whatever you ask for in prayer."
content: Jesus answers the question of why the fig tree withered quickly.Alison looked suspiciously at the women as they prayed in earnest.  She’s prayed before and nothing seemed to happen.  This was her first time with this group of women and she was weary.  She believes she’s seen it all and she was still skeptical.Each woman came and spoke from her heart about what God had done, what He is doing and what she anticipate He’ll do.  The question each testimony left tugging at her heart was, “Do you believe?” With tears streaming down her check she resolved to change and pray with confidence.  So she asks, “Do you believe?”

title: You're Crown
verse: “Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast which thou hast, that no man take thy crown.” - (Revelation 3:11)
content:
A portion of the letter to the church in Philadelphia.

We have been hearing for a long, long time; Jesus is coming back again, soon.  Many laugh at the saying because soon has been a long time coming.  

As Christians we are promised many rewards, let us hold fast to the faith we have in Christ until He returns.  

Quickly is relative; our understanding and interpretation is different from that of God’s, so hold  firm and you will receive your crown which is your just reward of endurance.
