Worry is a sin; it’s like a rocking chair that keeps us moving, but takes us nowhere.

The recipe for overcoming worry: learn to trust God and all that He does and allows.

We can’t change or control anything. God is trustworthy; He has a proven track record therefore we can trust Him and overcome worry.

title: Refreshing Comes
verse: (Acts 3:19)“Repent, then, and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped out, that times of refreshing may come from the Lord,”
content: A portion of Peter’s speech to the onlookers recorded in the book of Acts.If you been a woman for any length of time you’ll know that seasons come and seasons go; you’ve done good and you’ve done bad.  In some instance the bad outweighs the good and therefore we have need of repentance. It’s all too easy for us to let our emotions rule our days and nights and that is devastating. So we hang our heads shamefully and go to our Lord to take our sins away after which refreshing comes and then we can hold our heads high.

title: Rejoice
verse: “Rejoice in the Lord always. I will say it again: Rejoice!” (Philippians 4:4)
content: Paul encourages the Philippians not to be anxious but to think positive thoughts and to rejoice.Its seven days before Christmas…..is there anything to rejoice about? Don’t think about the things you don’t have or would like to have, think about the things you do have and of those who may be sick or are suffering. It is good to count our blessings from time to time; it keeps us grateful and humble.  Situations are not always conducive for rejoicing; it’s an art that we need to cultivate with the help of the Holy Spirit.If we think about it long enough we will realize there are more things going right than wrong therefore let’s REJOICE!

title: A Fruitful Witness
verse: “So faith comes from hearing, and hearing by the Word of God.” (Romans 10:17)
content: The Apostle Paul writes about Israel’s rejection of the Gospel.Are you living in a home with unsaved loved ones? Pushing and pulling them to come to faith is a vain effort. As women we have the unique capability of setting the atmosphere which we should do in our homes.  We set it by making God’s Word encouraging, exalted, ever-present, and ever-obeyed. Only God’s Word saves. The more it is heard in a favorable light, the more conducive the atmosphere will be for salvation by faith.  Therefore be diligent at it.It is not only God’s written word but also the “word” of our actions that acts as a fruitful witness.

title: Wisdom & Justice
verse: “The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice. “ - (Psalm 37:30)
content:
The misfortune of the wicked and the legacy of the righteous are depicted in this Psalm. 

The King makes a decree; his subjects do not understand why such a decree was made.  

He is wise, very insightful, always concerned about the wellbeing of the people, he is respectful; genuine and metes out justice for all.  The subjects are at peace, even though they do not understand this decree, they can rest in the assurance that it is in and for their best interest.

Likewise we too should utter wisdom and speak justice to edify and build those we come in contact with.
 
Wisdom and justice flows from a righteous woman such as you.

title: Attentive to your Prayer
verse: (1 Peter 3:12)“For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous and his ears are attentive to their prayer, but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”
content: Peter addresses in his writing the issue of suffering for doing good.As I think about prayer, the latest movie by the Kendricks brothers entitled “War Room” comes to mind.  It encourages fighting our battles on our knees in prayer; pleading with God and then allowing him to do what He does best.God is attentive to your prayer, to my prayer.  As a mother with adult children I know the value of prayer.  What do you pray about? God is attentive to every word you pray so keep on praying!

title: Wise or Foolish?
verse: “Five of them were foolish and five were wise. The foolish ones took their lamps but did not take any oil with them.  The wise ones, however, took oil in jars along with their lamps.” - (Matthew 25:2-4)
content:
Matthew records Jesus telling the parable of the ten virgins.
 
Alexander Graham Bell quotes, “Before anything else, preparation is the key to success.”

This well known parable innately speaks about preparation but there are other aspects as well, such as being wise or foolish.

If you are wise, you will make the necessary preparations for succeeding, if you are foolish however, everything will be left to chance and you will accomplish nothing, living in regret and with missed opportunities. 

Wise or foolish? The choice is yours to make.

title: Secret Giving
verse: (Matthew 6:4)“so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.”
content: Matthew writes what Jesus says about giving to the needy.A story is told of a woman who feeds everyone she can from her pot each weekend.  She has a heart for the less fortunate.  She doesn’t own a cook shop or is considered rich but she knows what it is to be hungry and what it feels like when your children tug at your skirt looks in your eyes and says, “Mommy I am hungry.”She practices secret giving - meeting the needs of others just in the nick of time. Giving to the needy will be rewarded, it will help others immensely.

title: No Joy in Sin
verse: “The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, that one may avoid the snares of death.”  (Proverbs 14:27)
content: God explains to us that obeying His Word leads to a fountain of life. Do your actions show that you believe this? Often, when God is confronts us about a sin we respond like He is leading us into the snare of death and not away from it. “God is robbing me of enjoyment, goodness, family, friends, or experience,” we might say. Our eyes become focused on what the world has and how seemingly peaceful their lives are. But like the Psalmist, we must remember the eternal damnation of those who sin without repentance. In faith, we must obey God and trust that He will reward us.

title: Rooted & Grounded
verse: “That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith; that ye, being rooted and grounded in love,” - (Ephesians 3:17)
content:
Paul prays for the Ephesians, that they may grasp the vastness of God’s love and grace.

Rooted and grounded readily brings to mind an old tree that has stood the test of time and weather.  Its roots go deep into the ground so that it is not easily toppled or unearthed. 

Christ dwells in our hearts by faith not by feeling; feeling is fickle. Faith settles who Christ is to us and makes us rooted and grounded like the old tree in His love. Our intimate relationship with Christ provides this guarantee.  His love and grace are limitless but they are enough to keep us.

title: Strength to Care
verse: "For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.” (2 Timothy 1:7)
content: Because of our natural nurturing capacity, women tend to be the “go-to” for some of the most difficult situations. If you are a caregiver you know the pressure of handling many issues without falling apart. When God tells us He has not equipped us with fear, but with power, love, and a sound mind, it reminds us that God is supplying our needs for every situation. We need not depend on ourselves, but depend on Him to give us power to endure, love to forgive, compassion where needed, and a sound mind to give advice and encouragement to those who depend on us most.

title: Growing Wise
verse: “He who walks with the wise grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.” - (Proverbs 13:20)
content:
A pearl regarding the wise and foolish; what becomes of each.

It’s been said that birds of a feather flock together. The next time you see a flock of birds flying together, you can check, they are the same kind of birds. You’re not likely to see sparrows, crows and seagulls flying in the same flock.

So too following the example of the birds we should “fly” with the wise in order to grow wise.  Refuse to be in the company of fools, their demise is tragic.

Growing wise requires concerted effort but the rewards are worth their weight in gold. The results will last for eternity. Flock with the wise.

title: Lineage of Respect
verse: “Honor your father and your mother…”  (Exodus 20:12)
content: If your parents are seeking to guide you in the way of the Lord, this is not a difficult commandment to follow. You take their advice, prayer, and even chastisement, in confidence. However, if your parents have worldly ideas, getting their advice might not always be to your wellbeing. Respect the fact that your parents are not able to give you that kind of guidance but seek to honor them in other ways. Ask them for help in their areas of expertise. Spend time with them and do things for them, let them know that you care about them. Above all, pray for them especially through their difficulties and make it a priority in your life.

title: Fruitful and Multiplying
verse: “But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance; against such there is no law.” (Galatians 5:22-23)
content: When the Spirit is truly present in our lives, it will be evident through the nine-fold fruit of the Spirit. Love	the act of sacrifice, service, and submission Joy	peaceful confidence in God, despite trials and difficult circumstancesPeace	commitment to resolutions of conflict, despite differences Longsuffering	persevering without resentmentGentleness	communicating with mercy and humility, verbally and non-verballyGoodness	strong moral characterFaith	hope with assuranceMeekness	humility before God and othersTemperance	restrictions and convictions that are maintained without waveringCan you recognize God’s presence in your life through the fruit of the Spirit? Be fruitful and multiply.

title: Buying Back
verse: “Do not fear, you worm Jacob, you men of Israel; I will help you, declares the Lord, and your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.”  (Isaiah 41:14)
content: Though the concept of Kinsman-Redeemer is foreign to most of the Western world, it played a significant role in shaping history. A kinsman-redeemer was a next of kin male who absorbed the debts and responsibilities of a widow. It required tremendous personal sacrifice. He owned nothing; his children were considered the children of his wife’s first husband. He wasn’t entitled to any kind of inheritance. When God promises to be the Kinsman-Redeemer of Israel, He bought her back because of His great love; He willingly paid that price and now offers redemption to all who will accept it.

title: Worship Him
verse: (Matthew 2:10-11)“When they saw the star, they were overjoyed. On coming to the house, they saw the child with his mother Mary, and they bowed down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold and of incense and of myrrh.”
content: Matthew’s account of the visit of the wise men at the time of Jesus’ birth.The season is not over, it has just begun.  Thinking about what Jesus’ birth means is enough reason to worship Him.  The wise men knew of His significance, they knew that He was the designated King and therefore they sought after Him until they found Him.We too should seek Him and worship Him first and foremost with our lives and our treasures.

title: Perseverance and Faith
verse: “ therefore, we ourselves speak proudly of you among the churches of God for your perseverance and faith in the midst of all your persecutions and afflictions which you endure.” (2 Thessalonians 1:4)
content: Paul tells about the Thessalonians’ faith that throughout their suffering and persecution they trusted Christ unwaveringly.They did not glory in their religious experiences, their history, their future, their possessions or their position of authority on earth. They gloried in Christ even when they were exhausted and frustrated. Likewise, our glory should be in nothing but the power of God that causes us to triumph.  It is through perseverance and faith in Him that we will be able to endure all persecutions and afflictions and become an example for others to follow.

title: Changing Every Day
verse: “But we know that when Christ appears, we will be like him, for we will see him as he is.”  (1 John 3:2)
content: The key to being transformed into Christ’s image is to see Him as He is. This will be accomplished fully when He returns, but while we wait for Him, we should be focused on Jesus – the Author and Finisher of our faith. Whom should we learn to see Christ as? We should see Him as the Creator (John 1:3), as Lord (Philippians 2:11), and as the only Savior from sin and the curse (John 3:16, Romans 8:21).  He is also the immortal God (Hebrews 13:8). Through regular time in His Word, we will learn His attributes and become more and more like Him.

title: A Shout
verse: “For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:” - (1 Thessalonians 4:16)
content:
Christ‘s second return and the resurrection of the saints.
 
Sometimes in our day to day encounters we would love to hear a shout that would alter our situation for better. Just one shout of authority to change things around a bit and give us some relief. 

Here Paul tells us that we can look forward to a shout from heaven when the Lord returns the second time. It will change things around too, those who have accepted Him as Lord and Savior will be resurrected.

Let’s anticipate the shout of the Lord.

title: Agreement
verse: “Can two walk together, except they be agreed?” - (Amos 3:3)
content:
Amos was a farmer in his day; to him social acceptance did not count. He brought God’s message to the people; here he asks a valid question.

Two cows are yoked to plow the farm and they are fighting each other to go their separate ways but the yoke constrains them.  

Unlike cows we have no yoke to constrain us.  Your relationships will not work unless both parties agree to commit to walking the same path at the same time. 

Think of your relationship with your parents, spouse, boss, girlfriend, pastor, insurance agent; unless you have a common goal in view you cannot walk together.  You must come into agreement to 
be successful in all your relationships.

title: Wolves and Sheep
verse: "Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ferocious wolves." (Matthew 7:15)
content: Jesus gives us a word picture here to demonstrate to us the need for spiritual discernment in the church. Men and women need to know the Scriptures and search them like the Bereans did, in order to accurately determine, whether the things being taught are true. Women can be supportive and instrumental with confronting and challenging bad doctrines.  This can only be done effectively however when they understand the doctrines themselves and learn God’s Word and what it says about dealing with doctrinal discords within the church. The importance and authority of God’s Word will be preserved and it will expose wolves in sheep’s clothing.

title: Fishers of Men
verse: (Matthew 4:19)" 'Come, follow me,' Jesus said, 'and I will make you fishers of men.' "
content: The calling of the first disciples as recorded in the book of Matthew.I hope that we can all remember what life was like before we began to follow Christ because it will be important as we ourselves become “fishers of men”.  Some of the disciples were fishermen by trade; they knew all about catching fish but Jesus introduces the idea of fishing for men. We too are called to be fishers of men and women by extension.  Reflecting on how life used to be before we met Christ, should motivate and make us eager to tell others about our new life and to pull them into the net of salvation.

title: Battling for your Children
verse: "Be on the alert, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.”  (1 Corinthians 16:13)
content: One of the hardest areas to stand fast in faith is with your children. There is the issue of Christian or secular education and it’s cost.   Also the issue of socializing, will it damage your children?There are choices to make regarding media and entertainment, but can you keep them engaged in something better for all of their waking hours? Temptations to compromise on Godly motherhood responsibilities are all around. In your most stressed moments, you are called to make difficult decisions, to stand firm in your convictions. Stand fast in your desire to raise your children for the Lord when easier alternatives are presented each day.

title: Path of Life
verse: “Thou wilt shew me the path of life: in thy presence [is] fullness of joy; at thy right hand [there are] pleasures for evermore.” - (Psalms 16:11)
content:
David writes about the hope of the faithful and of Jesus’ return.

The path of life consists of fullness of joy in God’s presence and pleasures at His right hand for all eternity.

A pathway can be lined with many things, good and bad, pleasant and unpleasant but like the Psalmist we ask God to show us “the path of life”. Not just any path, but the ultimate life-giving path; a path that is only found in God’s presence.

Use the stepping stones of encouragement, praise and worship, strength, humility, prayer and obedience to stay on the path of life each day.

title: Praise, Honor and Glory
verse: “so that the proof of your faith, being more precious than gold which is perishable, even through tested by fire, may be found to result in praise and glory and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ;” (1 Peter 1:7)
content: Every Christian’s faith will be tried. Being able to continually give praise, honor, and glory to the Lord will be a sacrifice.Various scripture speak of trials and its rewards; James 1:3-4 says that trials produces endurance, which will make us complete in Christ, lacking nothing, 1 Peter 4:12 and Romans 5:3 also speak of the rewards derived from enduring trails.Though trials seem overwhelming and impossible to overcome, God says in His Word that there is purpose in our trials and tribulations.

title: Good News
verse: (Luke 2:10)“And the angel said to them, “Fear not, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.”
content: The birth and childhood of Jesus as recorded in the book of Luke.We all like good news or rather we all prefer to hear good news any and every day of the week.  Here recorded in the book of Luke is the Good News of and for all times.Good news is a breath of fresh air that gives us hope and fills us with joy.  This good news is for all people of all time, what a blessing and encouragement!Take the good news of the Messiah’s coming and tell it to the world, they need to know.

title: No Fear
verse: “There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love.” - (1 John 4:18)
content:
John is writing about boldness and fear.

The antidote for fear is love.  

Oh, how you and I NEED something to counteract the unpleasant feeling of fear!  The fears that torment and paralyze us; holding us captive and unfulfilled. 

Suzan knows that she has walked out of line in her relationship with the Lord, she is ashamed but she also knows that no one else can help her.  She repents, goes back to the Father, trembling at first but pushing forward until there is no fear.  

She receives forgiveness and restoration, and you will too.

title: The Law of Kindness
verse: “She speaks with wisdom, and faithful instruction is on her tongue.” - (Proverbs 31:26)
content:
Taken from the classical description of a Noble Wife, A Virtuous Woman, this attribute is listed among the qualities of this remarkable woman.

It might make you wonder if the description of this woman is practical and attainable by the simplest of women.  Don’t let that intimidate you, it is attainable!  

The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.

Ensure that when you open your mouth to speak, positive words flow out. This is the first step.  Wisdom flows when you are positive, seeking to build up and not to destroy.  Many are waiting for the wisdom and instructions that are in you, so speak up.

title: Sacrificing Entitlement
verse: "Anyone who loves his father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; anyone who loves his son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me; and anyone who does not take his cross and follow me is not worthy of me. Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for my sake will find it." (Matthew 10:37-39)
content: “I deserve it!”Have you ever said those words? We value ourselves based on the sacrifices we have made or the injustices we have experienced.      Sacrifice seems to invoke a sense of entitlement and establishes idols in our lives that we run to instead of Christ for comfort.      Let the “cross” you bear bring you closer to Christ who carried the Cross for you.

title: Rest in the Lord
verse: “Rest in the LORD, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.” - (Psalms 37:7)
content:
The Psalmist writes about the heritage of the righteous.

I am sure you’ve seen older women whose hairs are lined with grey, unperturbed about what is happening around them and, they are not senile. They will tell you that they know who they are and whose they are.  They belong to Jesus so they rest in the Lord because He takes care of all things in His time.

At whatever age we are, let us catch the concept of resting in the Lord; he takes care of evil doers so wait patiently for Him.

title: Prosperity
verse:
“…but whose delight is in the law of the Lord, and who meditates on his law day and night.
That person is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season
and whose leaf does not wither—whatever they do prospers.” - (Psalm 1:2-3)
content:
This Psalm tells what will happen to the ungodly and highlights the way of the righteous.

You’ve watched sisters in the women’s ministry work diligently at serving and living by the principles of God’s word.  As you remember, it was their actions that propelled and influenced you to join the ministry.  You too have embraced the same principles and service etiquette which has borne fruit for every person.

Prosperity is the reward, therefore you encourage others to “follow suit.”

title: Divided, We Fall
verse: "Every kingdom divided against itself will be ruined, and every city or household divided against itself will not stand." (Matthew 12:25)
content: Proverbs says that the contentious woman tears down her own house. Women become contentious when they are hurt and want to retaliate. This results in the division of their household and instead of satisfaction, hurt is inflicted. Deep down in her heart she hopes that her efforts will change her family and make them better. The problem however is that like Sarah, she tries to accomplish God’s will in her own manipulative way. And instead of peace, she brings division and demise to her home. Be careful not to tear down your home by inflicting hurts, but enrich it with joy throughout suffering and patience in tribulation.

title: Perfected Love
verse: “No man hath seen God at any time. If we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and his love is perfected in us.” - (1 John 4:12)
content:
This chapter speaks of God’s love for us, our love for God and for others.

The older woman, Catherine embraces Julia for a moment, when she frees her Julia smiles.

Julia came to live in the community a year ago, she had lost much of her belongings in a fire but today she is safe, confident and happier than she had been in a long time.  They became friends shortly after moving to the community.  

Catherine doesn’t preach the Bible to Julia but Julia now sees God through her in a tangible way. 

May God’s love be perfected in us.

title: Living Sacrifice
verse: “I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, [which is] your reasonable service.” - (Romans 12:1)
content:
Paul implores the Romans to live transformed lives.

In olden days, a lamb without spot or blemish was the only acceptable sacrifice to give. Fast forward to modern day; your life is the ultimate sacrifice to give to God.

Keeping yourself sexually pure, your thoughts pleasing to God, repenting on a regular basis, establishing proper eating habits, exercising; doing these things and others, make your life a living sacrifice to God.
He will accept that from you as your reasonable service. It’s not about a sacrifice on an altar any more, but a lifestyle that pleases God.

title: Ministry of Music
verse: “You are my hiding place; You preserve me from trouble; You surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.” (Psalm 32:7)
content: The psalmist expresses the joy of forgiveness.God intended for music to play a special role in our lives. Scientific research supports and has shown how music makes significant changes in our brain and body, for better or worse. Music helps us to visualize God's presence, or prompts us to “feel” His absence. It can be full of good doctrine, like the songs sung by Vicki Yohe, and others, or it can be a deceptive device of Satan. It can calm your mind, or distract you, give you peace or fear. Let God minster to you through music that will deliver and make you whole.

title: Heavenly City
verse: “But now they desire a better [country], that is, a heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.” - (Hebrews 11:16)
content:
A verse from the passage entitled “The Honor Roll of the Faithful.”

Abel, Enoch, Noah, Abraham, and Sara are listed in this honor roll.  They died in faith as pilgrims sojourning to the heavenly city that God prepared for them.

They were the “greats”, schooled in the halls of faithfulness but don’t be perplexed; you too have the hope like these great men and women of entering the heavenly city. 

Your desire for a better place will be materialized, your place has been prepared and it is secure. 

You too are faithful in your own way.

title: To Be Wise
verse: “Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD and turn away from evil.” - (Proverbs 3:7)
content:
Proverbs tells us how to be wise from God’s perspective.

Sally opened her eyes and groaned before she got out of bed.  Her grandmother cautioned her yesterday about her actions towards Tara, her friend from middle school.  Etta, her grandmother told her to build the friendship as it would benefit them both in years to come.

Grandma was right, Sally knew and so she did not act upon her own opinion, she shunned the evil thoughts that fueled the discontent that was brewing in her mind. 

Today they are still friends who have enhanced each other’s lives.

To be wise, we must turn away from evil and fear the Lord.

title: Working for God
verse: “Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for men,” - (Colossians 3:23)
content:
This section of Colossians is titled: Character of the Christian life. It is a rule for holy living.

No matter how they laugh at you, scorn or scoff at you, call you an idiot, you still keep doing the right thing.  You show up for work on time after you’ve being up all night with a sick child, you prepare your husband’s meals even though he’s having an affair and you clock in your normal volunteer hours although you are not recognized for what you do.

It hurts sometimes, but keep working for God regardless of what men say or do, He is your rewarder.

title: Wisdom
verse: “Wisdom belongs to the aged, and understanding to the old.” - (Job 12:12)
content:
Job’s answer to his critics.

Not all aged and old people are wise or understanding, it is derived from wisdom.  Usually if you are wise, you are considered old and if you are old, you should be wise.

From the traumas of sickness and disease, Margaret has watched Helen rise and fall. Helen has battled several illness and came through a debilitating disease, fibromyalgia.  Margaret knows that Helen is not “old” but she is wise beyond her years.

Helen deals with her situation by appreciating the sovereignty of God and how He handles the affairs of men and this gives her wisdom.

We can rest in the knowledge of God’s power and wisdom, that’s what Helen does.

title: Great Faith
verse: “Then Jesus answered, "Woman, you have great faith! Your request is granted." And her daughter was healed from that very hour.” (Matthew 15:28)
content: The faith of the Canaanite woman and Jesus’ response.If you’re a mother, you know the pain that comes when your child is ill, and as a child you’ve experience the tender care of a mother.It requires great faith in the midst of adversity, to believe God for your children at every stage of their lives.  Whatever your child is doing or not doing, have faith that God will see them through, whether they’ve lived up to what you have taught them or not.  Like this woman, exercise your faith and trust God to heal them, bless them and set them free.

title: Sun and Shield
verse: (Psalms 84:11)“For the LORD God is a sun and shield; the LORD bestows favor and honor. No good thing does he withhold from those who walk uprightly.”
content: This Psalms record the blessings that come with dwelling in God’s house.I think its classic that God is described here in this Psalm as a sun and as a shield. A shield provides protection and we need protection from the sun sometimes.But let’s move beyond that aspect however.  God is a sun, providing light and warmth and He is also a shield that protects us from harm and danger.What a mighty God is He! He is a sun and shield who gives favor and honor and gives good things to those who walk uprightly.

title: The Recompense of God
verse: “Say to those with anxious heart, ‘Take courage, fear not. Behold, your God will come with vengeance;The recompense of God will come, But He will save you.’ ” (Isaiah 35:4)
content: The Prince of the Prophets, Isaiah writes about the future glory of Zion.In this day and age it is very easy to become anxious about one thing or another.  These words are a welcome encouragement! God will come with vengeance, He will compensate for the losses and harm we have suffered and for the efforts we have made.  That is the recompense of God.I rejoice and so should you. God is with us, He knows EVERY thing that has happened and He will deal with each of them. Take courage and discard fear!

title: Feeling or Faith?
verse: “When I am afraid, I will put my trust in You.”  (Psalm 56:3)
content: There are times in our life when we can honestly say that we do not feel like trusting God. Fear, anger, or hatred is seemingly more satisfying and easier than trusting God. Yet the Psalmist does not say that when trusting is easy or when we feel like we will put our trust in Him. Instead, it is when it is hardest, when our minds are clouded with doubt, and when our emotions are awry, we will choose to trust God. What is keeping you from putting your faith in God? When the odds are stacked against you, you can trust Him and rest assured that He will not disappoint you.

title: Cause for Rejoicing
verse: “It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.” - (Luke 15:32)
content:
The parable of the Lost Son recorded by Luke; told by Jesus.

We know how it feels when we lose something and then find it; it makes us happy and celebratory, especially if it is something dear to us.

So too in the Kingdom of God, when a sinner turns or returns to the Kingdom, it is cause for rejoicing! 

Angels in heaven rejoice over one sinner that repents so we too join in the rejoicing.  Being in sin makes us dead but coming to Christ makes us alive.  

Let us wholeheartedly welcome everyone who comes to the Kingdom.

title: Prophet's Reward
verse: “He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man's reward.” - (Matthew 10:41)
content:
Jesus’ instructions to His disciples regarding missions.

Let’s recount the story of Elijah and the Widow of Zarephath as recorded in 1 Kings 17:7-16.  

There was a famine in the land, Elijah instructs this widow to make him a cake and bring him water to drink.  From the meager supplies she has for her son and herself, she obliges and the rest is history, as the saying goes. She receives more than she could ever imagine; a prophet’s reward.

To receive these rewards requires submission and obedience.

title: Name of the Peaceable
verse: “Blessed are the peacemakers for they shall be called the children of God.”  (Matthew 5:9)
content: Let’s face it, women love drama; they love a good story and a bit of gossip. And as much as it drives us crazy, our children are following in our footsteps of creating chaos. From their messes to fights with siblings, it seems peace is a million miles away from our homes. Our inclination to drama separates us from that peace we desperately crave. To have peace with each other, we must first have peace with God by reuniting with Him.Then, we must remind ourselves and our children to imitate God’s actions in our relationships. In so doing, we direct our families and ourselves away from being chaos-creators to peacemakers.

title: New Heaven and Earth
verse: “And I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away; and there was no more sea.” - (Revelation 21:1)
content:
John’s vision of Christ in the New Jerusalem.

The book of revelation can be confusing, but it ends with Christ as the victor and sometimes that is all you really need to know and understand.

This new heaven and earth will not be consumed with sin as we know it now.  It’s something to look forward to after all that we have seen and experienced on this present earth.

The old will no longer be relevant; the new will rule and dominate.  

There will be no wars, no death, and peace forevermore in this new heaven and earth.

title: Fear & Honor
verse: “Honour all [men]. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honour the king.” - (1 Peter 2:17)
content:
Peter’s words about submission to those in authority.

To fear God is, awe, terror, reverence, wonder, respect for who He is and what He can do. It is a good thing for us to fear Him because it causes us to relinquish our sinful nature and work towards holiness.

High respect with great esteem is how we should honor men and kings.  However, they cannot be compared to God who created man and the universe.

It will be in our best interest to distinguish between fear and honor and to whom it is due.  It’s not complicated; fear God, love people, honor all men and those in authority.

title: Your Song
verse: “And Mary said: My soul glorifies the Lord and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior, for he has been mindful of the humble state of his servant. From now on all generations will call me blessed,” - (Luke 1:46-48)
content:
Mary had been chosen to be Jesus’ Mother and this was her response, which is widely known as Mary’s Song of Praise.

Can you image? Little, humble, insignificant you, have been chosen above all the other women, who were more than qualified and they have the credentials to prove it.  

But it was YOU that was chosen; you don’t understand it and you don’t need to, it’s called God’s favor.  So, open your mouth and sing Your Song of Praise to God!

title: Like Sarah
verse: “Even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose daughters ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any amazement.” - (1 Peter 3:6)
content:
Peter recounts Sara calling Abraham lord in the Old Testament. 

The act of Sarah calling Abraham lord was an act of submission and obedience.  

Submission is a word that many of us as women cringe at because it has been overly stated and misused. 

You don’t need to be married to encounter submission, we should submit to those in authority.

Godly submission is faith that God will work through your authority to accomplish what is best for you.

So like Sarah, be submissive and obedient and see how God will work things out for your good.

title: Hiding Place
verse: (Psalm 32:7-8)“You are my hiding place; you will protect me from trouble and surround me with songs of deliverance.”
content: The joy of forgiveness is expressed by the Psalmist.The words, hiding place reminds me of the stirring movie I watched about Corrie Ten Boom. She was a Dutch Christian who, along with her father and other family members, helped many Jews escape the Nazi Holocaust during World War 2. They were eventually imprisoned for what they did.Our God – our all protecting hiding place, will NEVER be caught or imprisoned.  He will always be the hiding place, which protects from trouble and surround us with songs of deliverance.We can take refuge in this hiding place, depending solely on God’s grace and mercy as we trust Him unswervingly.

title: Born Again
verse: “In reply Jesus declared, "I tell you the truth, no one can see the kingdom of God unless he is born again."” - (John 3:3)
content:
Jesus teaches Nicodemus.

Our first birth came from our mother’s womb.  Being born again comes through the Holy Spirit: acceptance of Christ as Lord and Savior.

Getting saved is the first step to being born again.  You now have a Heavenly Father and a new family of like-minded believers.  The term, a Child of God becomes applicable.  You learn to trust in Jesus Christ and depend upon the Holy Spirit to live this new life.

It is a blessing to be born again into such a unique family; you are heir to privileges such a being able to see the Kingdom of God.  Welcome!

title: Another Chance
verse: “God, my God, I yelled for help and you put me together.  God, you pulled me out of the grave, gave me another chance at life when I was down-and-out.”(Psalm 30:2-3)
content: This is a desperate cry from the Psalmist David for himself and on behalf of his people whenever they were afflicted.   God is our maker and we turn to Him when in distress.As women, emotional distress is a real and present factor that we deal with on a daily basis.  To be victorious in this feat, we like David must turn to God who created us to help, guide and direct us to emotional wholeness.  We are afforded another chance when we turn to Him; He pulls us up and heals our afflictions.

title: Our Advocate
verse: “My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. And if any man sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous:” - (1 John 2:1)
content:
An affectionate appeal written by John the Apostle to be righteous but explains also that if failure occurs we are not doomed.  
 
Jesus Christ our advocate: comfort, attorney.  How often have we failed, hung our heads in shame and wondered in disbelief? Our walk of righteousness is filled with stumbling blocks but that should not deter us from getting up and continuing the journey.  

We are assured in this passage that we have an advocate who fights on our behalf, who holds our 
hands and leads us on.

title: Pure Heart
verse: “Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and [of] a good conscience, and [of] faith unfeigned:” - (1 Timothy 1:5)
content:
Paul’s command against false teachers of the law.
 
To: You
From: God

Cultivate love from a pure heart as you journey through life each day; avoid malice, treachery and the likes. Embrace good ethics and operate in sincere faith.  Avoid all other doctrines that are not from me, they are just fables which will lead you astray.

My daughter you are precious in my sight so harken unto my words, I desire the best for you.

I am the beginning, the end and I’m in the middle as well, I see and know all things. I am with you always.

title: The Mind of Christ
verse: “Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus:” - (Philippians 2:5)
content:
Paul recommends humility for the believer.

Jenny knew what she was doing but she’s heard it before, “You’re too spiritual”.

She ponders within herself if that is truly so.  What she does is replicate the prototype that changed her life completely; she owes her sanity and dignity to Him. 

Jesus Christ is her example. She isn’t equal to Him in anyway, she never will be on this side of eternity but she humbles herself to imitating His character and to doing His will by His standards.  She trains her mind in the Word of God in order to cultivate the mind of Christ.

Ignore the criticism, develop the mind of Christ, Jenny says.

title: Love of Neighbor
verse: “For all the law is fulfilled in one word, [even] in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.” - (Galatians 5:14)
content:
A repeat of the Old Testament passage, Leviticus 19:18, imploring love of neighbor.

The definition for love of self is, the instinct by which one’s actions are directed to the promotion of one’s welfare or well-being, especially an excessive regard for one’s advantage.

Love of neighbor means respecting their boundary, blessing them, being a good listener, not stealing from them, holding no grudge against them, not being deceptive in dealing with them, treating them as how you want to be treated.

Just as we love and would work to achieve goals for ourselves we are hereby asked to do the same thing for our neighbor.

title: Inheritance of the Poor
verse: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.”  (Matthew 5:3)
content: What does it mean to be “poor in spirit”? A woman who is poor in spirit grieves over her sin instead of blaming people and circumstances for making her the way she is. When trials beset her, she uses them to examine her relationship with God and does not become resentful and sour. She takes Godly counsel before making a decision instead of trusting that she already knows the answers. She seeks to learn from her Savior above all others. She looks to Him to meet her needs, instead of trying to do things her own way.  As such, the gift of the kingdom of heaven is her inheritance.

title: Nothing Separates
verse: “For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.” - (Romans 8:38-39)
content:
Paul’s assurance to the Romans and to us that we are more than conquerors through Jesus Christ.

As an umbilical cord attaches a fetus to its mother, so are we attached to God and NOTHING can or will be able to pull us away from Him.

Whatever may come to your mind, it does not have the right to unseat you from your position; nothing separates you from the love of God!

title: Do not Judge
verse: “Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God.” - (1 Corinthians 4:5)
content:
An admonishment from Paul regarding the rebellion of the Church in Corinth. 

Nothing is hidden from God, He knows EVERYTHING including a person’s heart which we cannot see.  At best we presume from what we can see and what we understand based on our limited knowledge. 

Paul is asking us to trust God to reveal all things in His time; He will address every matter that pertains to us, therefore do not judge.

Rest easy, don’t be perturbed, God’s eyes are looking, praise Him.

title: An Overcomer
verse: “Who is he that overcometh the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?” - (1 John 5:5)
content:
The benefit of faith, in the Son of God, to overcome the world.

You might look at your life and think you are a failure, but that is a lie.  Every day when you get out of bed and face the world you are an overcomer.  It doesn’t matter how many times you’ve failed or fallen, who hasn’t?

The truth is you are getting better in your struggles, you defeat the enemy; you triumph over adversities and become a little stronger each time.  

Lift up your head, thank God for the strength that He has given you through Jesus which enables you to overcome the world.

title: The Coronation
verse: “Blessed is a man who perceivers under trial; for once he had been approved, he will receive the crown of life which the Lord has promised to those who love Him.”  (James 1:12)
content: James illuminates the testing our faith and the reward for endurance through the test.  Notice that this verse is a promise to those that endure temptation, not to those who escape it. Women who seek easy fixes for every pain and escape routes by giving in will find themselves without this crown. When we endure trials and when we refuse to bow to the sins we are tempted with, we will receive an eternal reward-in-waiting; the crown of life. Through endurance, we are building a witness of our love and dedication for Christ.

title: Comfort
verse: “As a mother comforts her child, so I will comfort you; and you will be comforted over Jerusalem.” - (Isaiah 66:13)
content:
Written by the prophet Isaiah, he writes about God’s plans for the people and also shows the nature of God.

“I will comfort you”, says God,“and you will be comforted over your situations.”

Whatever ails you today the promise in today’s scripture is that God will bring you comfort.

As children, we remember how good it felt to climb up in our mother’s arms where she would rock us and or sing us a lullaby, especially after an unpleasant ordeal.

Here it is, just as a mother would, God will comfort us, He knows EVERYTHING about our situation and it doesn’t perplex Him. Embrace His comfort.

title: Problems with Perfectionism
verse: "Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect." (Matthew 5:48)
content: Perfection has been a temptation for women since the Garden of Eden. When God commands us here in His Word to be perfect, there is the stipulation: “as your heavenly Father is perfect”. We are not to seek perfection in our appearance, in our lifestyle, in our diet, in people or in accomplishments.  God wants us to seek perfection and completion in Him.He is the only perfect being. When we put on His image, God sees us through the Blood of His Son. This certainly is encouraging!But to be truly complete and perfect in God, we must conform daily to the image of Christ through humility, repentance of sin, and a mind saturated in His Word.

title: Temptation is the Trouble
verse: "Watch and pray so that you will not fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the body is weak."  (Matthew 26:41)
content: Jesus tells his disciples in Gethsemane to watch and pray; as recounted by Matthew.Notice in this verse that Jesus does not say, “Watch and pray that you will not fall into sin”, but He says, “Watch and pray that you will not fall into temptation.” God wants you to steer clear of atmospheres that are filled with temptations. Are you spiritually strong enough to withstand temptations? Jesus says that while our spirit is willing to obey, our body is weak.  To be victorious and strong in your bodies over temptation, you must watch and pray.Watch and pray so you do not fall.

title: The Teaching Woman
verse: “Whoever then annuls one of the least of these commandments, and teaches others to do the same, shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven: but whoever keeps and teach them, he shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.”  (Matthew 5:19)
content: Women have a special ministry of leading children and younger women to Christ. Those young people are watching the decisions and commitments made to form concepts of truth from what they see. If we ignore or believe contrary to the Word in any area, we will teach those looking on to do likewise. If we diligently apply the Word to every area of our lives; daily studies, and instructing others in truth, then we will be the recipients of great rewards in heaven.

title: Mind Control
verse: “And Jesus answered saying unto them, Have faith in God.” (Mark 11:22)
content: Mark the evangelist, records Jesus’ reply to His disciples to have unwavering confidence and faith in God, after the evidence that the fig tree He cursed had dried up.Many people flippantly express some kind of faith in God, but the Greek word translated here as “have” carries a much stronger meaning than a verbal profession; it means to control your mind. What Jesus is saying here is to let your faith in God control your mind. Whether you are surveying the wonders of creation, or struggling through a difficult time, it is your faith that should lead your thoughts. Are you meeting joys and sorrows with faith, or is your faith a passive expression?

title: Poor But Rich
verse: (James 2:5)“Listen, my dear brothers and sisters: Has not God chosen those who are poor in the eyes of the world to be rich in faith and to inherit the kingdom he promised those who love him?”
content: James forbids favoritism among the people.Faith is a wonderful gift; it transcends race, status stature and possessions.  The Kingdom of God works differently from the world system. James gives us a glimpse of how the Kingdom works; this verse tells us the “poor” in the eyes of the world are “rich” in faith and inherits the Kingdom.  Poor but rich in the Kingdom and sight of God is how it’s described here.   If you consider yourself poor be of good courage, you are rich in God.

title: Speech
verse: “For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.” - (Romans 10:10)
content:
The book of Romans summarizes the Christian faith.

It’s an average day, nothing unusual has happened.  Someone walks up and says something to you.  You open your mouth in reply and were embarrassed by the response you gave.  

Where did that come from?  Was that really me?

You know what just happened?  

What you had in your heart come through your mouth. But it is so very different when it comes to our salvation; the works begins in the heart so when we open our mouths we affirm our salvation. Our speech matches our heart’s intent.
The confession of our mouth affirms God’s goodness of salvation.

title: Be Humble
verse: “Humble yourselves, therefore, under God's mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time.  Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.” - (1 Peter 5:6-7)
content:
Peter’s own experience of suffering – imprisonment and flogging makes him authentic to give counsel on endurance. 

Women over the years have experienced discriminations in one form or other, at the hands of humanity.  We can however abandon ourselves in God’s care with the assurance of being dealt with justly.  

Continue to do fulfill your obligations, work your regular hours and take care of your family even if you are not recognized for it. God see and knows what you have done and what you are doing. Remain humble and He will lift you up!

title: Vision of the Pure
verse: “Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.”  (Matthew 5:8)
content: Purity begins in our hearts. Often through Scripture, God relates our sin to the act of adultery. As Christian women, we are “married” to Christ. Though we may not have committed physical adultery, we have committed spiritual adultery by giving our love and commitment to idols. Spiritual purity means having a single focused commitment and relationship with God, without dedication to idols. The pure in heart are single-minded in their pursuit of pleasing God with their body, mind and soul. The best of life from the world’s viewpoint is nothing to be compared with the happiness experienced by the pure in heart, who are satisfied with the promise to see God.

title: Manipulation
verse: “Do not let your heart envy sinners, but live in the fear of the Lord always.”  (Proverbs 23:17)
content: Women are able to manipulate their husbands, their children, their bosses and seemingly the whole world! With a snap of the finger, a pout of the lips, or a sway of the hips, women of the world seem to get the dream life. It is difficult not to look at them enviously and think, “If I just copied a little bit of what they do, I would get the respect and opportunities too.” But this verse exhorts us to fear God always. Through every trial we must remember that God is there and we should respond in a way that pleases Him and not allow our hearts to envy sinners.

title: Among the Wise
verse: “He whose ear listens to the life-giving reproof will dwell among the wise.” - (Proverbs 15:31)
content:
A wise person’s character includes willingness to be corrected.

Gena doesn’t say much these days but she is a keen listener.  She prefers to learn from the mistakes that other people make, than to learn by making them herself.  

She takes criticisms in strides.  Many persons think she is very wise by the decisions she make and by how she lives on a day to day basis.

Here’s what she has learnt:
a)	Change comes by deliberate action 
b)	Deal with the advice, not the feelings

Dwelling among the wise requires practice and diligence, dismissing feelings of hurt and shame, working at the advice that will change life and
implementing it.

title: The Mighty Savior
verse: For the Lord your God is living among you. He is a mighty savior. He will take delight in you with gladness. With his love, he will calm all your fears. He will rejoice over you with joyful songs.  (Zephaniah 3:17)
content: Zephaniah writes about Jerusalem’s sin, redemption and future.Can you imagine God living in your house? That would be an awesome thing.  Here in this scripture amidst sin and the prospect of redemption, God tells them that He’s living among them.The Mighty Savior takes delight in you gladly, His love will calm every fear that you have and He rejoices over you with joyful songs. What comforting words!Acknowledge your sins daily; repent quickly so you’ll be redeemed and God can live among you.

title: Come and Drink
verse: (John 7:37)“On the last and greatest day of the festival, Jesus stood and said in a loud voice, “Let anyone who is thirsty come to me and drink.”
content: Jesus teaches at the Feast.I watched the kids after they had played for awhile on the field run to the cooler to get water to quench their thirst.  If you watch long distance races you’ll see water stops along the way for the purposes of quenching thirst and rehydration as well.So too for us as we run the race of life we must take occasional stops to quench our thirst and rehydrate our bodies.  The invitation is clear, “come and drink”.  He will quench every thirst; and strengthen you throughout the journey of life.

title: Sons of God
verse: “Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.” - (1 John 3:2)
content:
John’s exposition of the Son’s of God and the command to love.

Sons of God: an exquisite terminology and that is how we are perceived by God when we accept Christ as Savior and Lord.  It is an awesome privilege and gives us such unique benefits.  

Even though we don’t know exactly what we shall be, we are certain that when Christ comes, we shall be like Him, seeing Him as He is.  We’ll be just like Him; we are the Sons of God.

title: Living Water
verse: (John 7:38)“Whoever believes in me, as Scripture has said, rivers of living water will flow from within them.”
content: Matthew’s record of Jesus’ teaching in the temple.Some benefits of water to the physical body include:•	Helps in digestion and constipation•	Flushes out Toxins•	Promotes healthy skin•	Regulates body temperature•	Proper blood circulation Now imagine “living” water flowing from our bodies.  If intake of water has such benefits, how much more beneficial will be that which flows out of us!Water a powerful source and element, vital for survival.  Our belief in Jesus qualifies us to be purifying and survival agents to those around us.  Let the word of God and His love flow as living water to everyone we meet.

title: Love Is
verse: “Love is patient, love is kind, and is not jealous; love does not brag and is not arrogant, does not act unbecomingly; it does not seek its own, is not provoked, does not take into account a wrong suffered, does not rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth; bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.” - (1 Corinthians 13:4-7)
content:
Paul writes about the Greatest Spiritual Gift - Love

Love is, not was, not going to be. Love is.  It’s an action word, actively doing, seeking to fulfill the interest of others, it’s not centered on self, it’s others centered. 

Think of the love of a parent for a child, regardless of how the child behaves, does or doesn’t, love covers and conquers it all.

title: Abounding Love
verse: “And this I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and [in] all judgment;” - (Philippians 1:9)
content:
Paul writes to the people of Philippi from a jail cell.

As we become knowledgeable about the person we love, our love grows when it is positive and diminishes when it is negative.

Abounding love in knowledge that Paul speaks of here is based on a spiritual understanding from God’s perspective, used correctly in our relationship with others.

As we learn more about God’s love, we love him more in return and likewise should love others more. Love in our relationships should be reflective of God’s love so that when we encounter difficulties, we trust His love in us to react graciously in knowledge and judgment.

title: Throw it Away
verse: (Matthew 5:29)"If your right eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell."
content: Jesus addresses the issue of adultery.I read a book recently that had a chapter titled: “Kill what kills you”, I was most intrigued.  The essence of it all is, before you let it kill you, go ahead and kill it first!Although Jesus was addressing adultery in this passage it most certainly relates to every bad habit that needs to be broken.What is causing you to stumble? Throw it away!  Don’t allow everything else that you have achieved be lost because of this one habit.

title: Your Mind
verse: “Wherefore gird up the loins of your mind, be sober, and hope to the end for the grace that is to be brought unto you at the revelation of Jesus Christ;” - (1 Peter 1:13)
content:
Peter tells us to be holy.

Gird up the loins of your mind: to prepare it to do something difficult and challenging from a worldly to a Godly view.

The mind is like a powerful computer that stores our memory and controls how we think and react. It is the center of the human nervous system, controlling our thoughts, movements, memories and decisions.

The only way to gird the mind as Peter says is by concerted effort, mediating and memorizing the Word of God.  

Your mind steers your whole body, channel it the right way.

title: I Am
verse: “Jesus said to them, ‘Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was born, I am.’ ” (John 8:58)
content: John records the claims of Jesus about Himself.A mother’s love is constant: past, present and future; her dedication falls short in the frailty of humanness but Jesus lives up to and delivers without fail.Here Jesus declares His sovereign omnipresence:  I AM…before time began, before Abraham was born…I AM.“I am” is present tense; happening now, not in the past or for the future and yet looking back over life we can trace God’s presence with us every step of the way.Go ahead, take I AM to the bank and cash it. Nothing or no one else is like Him; He changes not – guaranteed!

title: The Foundation of your Faith
verse: “That your faith should not stand in the wisdom of man, but in the power of God.”  (1 Corinthians 2:5)
content: It is a blessed gift to have spiritual union in your marriage. However, it can become a default for wives who rely on their husband’s knowledge and faith instead of studying God’s Word and depending on Him for themselves. The nature of our sin is too great a responsibility to pass on to your husband to deal with. Sin can only be dealt with through Christ on an individual basis. Knowing God’s Word for ourselves is the only way to be able to rightly divide the Word of Truth (2 Timothy 2:15) and to communicate a scriptural reason for your faith in God.

title: From Above
verse: “But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, unwavering, without hypocrisy.” - (James 3:17)
content:
Exploring two types of wisdom in the book of James. 

Martha can relate, she knew what it was like to take care of two children as a single mother.  She knew the heartache and adjustments that had to be made to survive.  

The wisdom she gained from years of being a single mother is now poured out into the lives of other women who are currently walking that journey.  She helps, counsels, feeds, and teaches them through this curve in life.

An awesome wonder indeed how her life has evolved, she’ll smile and tell you, “Your life can evolve too!”

title: Teaching
verse: “Likewise, teach the older women to be reverent in the way they live, not to be slanderers or addicted to much wine, but to teach what is good.” - (Titus 2:3)
content:
In the book of Titus, Paul addresses the diverse groups in the church; His pastoral advice is based on sound doctrine to help with real-life human problems.

Women go through many stages in life.  You grow from a young woman to an old woman. As an older woman you have made a few blunders, gained some experience, learnt a few things and have a sense of discernment.  

At this stage, develop a lifestyle of reverence, stay away from evil and strive to teach what you have learnt to another person so that their lives can be enriched.

title: Knocking
verse: “Behold, I stand at the door and knock; if anyone hears My voice and opens the door, I will come in to him and will dine with him, and he with Me.”(Revelation 3:20)
content: John writes about the lukewarm church, Laodicea.To enter a room you must go through a door.  Until the occupant opens the door the person knocking cannot enter.Jesus is knocking at the door heart of our hearts. For some He’s been knocking for a very long time, and for others He has just began.You MUST give him access by opening the door.  He wants to dine with you.  It’s not just about eating food, He wants to fellowship with you.Will you open the door of your heart to Him today?

title: Not Forgotten
verse: (Jeremiah 50:5)“They will ask for the way to Zion, turning their faces in its direction; they will come that they may join themselves to the LORD in an everlasting covenant that will not be forgotten.”
content: Jeremiah writes a message about Babylon. Tucked away in the attic, Jane found memorabilia and heirlooms that belonged to her family.  As she went through them her eyes swelled with tears; there was so much hidden and forgotten among the things she found.Then her thoughts turned to the God of her salvation and a smile peeps at the corners of her mouth.  She knows that He never forgets.Not forgotten; He will not forget those with whom He has made a covenant, who had joined themselves to Him.

title: Being Wise
verse: “Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands.” - (Proverbs 14:1)
content:
An astounding pearl of wisdom for the wise woman, regarding the ways of life and how to avoid being foolish.

A woman’s role in whatever capacity she fills carries an onus of influence.  She must be very careful then to build up and not tear down.  It is all too easy to be swayed by emotions that ebb and flow depending on the given circumstances.  

You must be very careful therefore to be a builder of strong beautiful structures, be it at home, at work, at school, at church, as a volunteer or in ministry. Let your hands refrain from plucking in your environment.

title: Adopted
verse: "For you did not receive a spirit that makes you a slave again to fear, but you received the Spirit of Sonship. And by him we cry, "Abba," Father."(Romans 8:15)
content: Adoption paints a powerful picture of salvation. God willfully chose us, paid the sum of His own life, and made us joint-heirs with His Son.To seal this for us, God has put His own Spirit in our hearts. With the Holy Spirit, we are free from the entrapment of sin. Whether you have experienced a physical or spiritual adoption, realize the amazing promise that God gives us through the Spirit and His Word. We are no longer slaves but “sons”.Through the Spirit of Sonship and adoption, embrace a new Father: Abba - God the Father, our Father!

title: Undivided Heart
verse: (2 Chronicles 19:9)“You must always act in the fear of the Lord, with faithfulness and an undivided heart.”
content: Jehoshaphat appoints Judges for the people.It’s difficult in our fast paced world to cultivate an undivided heart but the benefits are phenomenal.A heart that is undivided is powerful, it focuses on purpose singularly. With such a conviction,perseverance will prevail no matter the difficulty faced.  It is a sense of loyalty that will let you attempt and do the impossible for God.Move past the trivialities of life, reverence the Lord with fear and exercise faithfulness. It does not matters the state of your heart now, pray for the cultivation of an undivided heart that will transform your life, circumstances and the world in singleness of purpose.

title: Good Courage
verse: “Yes, we are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.” - (2 Corinthians 5:8)
content:
The assurance of resurrection and an expressed yearning to be with the Lord in heaven.

We should be of good courage in our present state of being although we would rather be with Christ in eternity.

Our hope of resurrection is the trigger for that courage. To be in the Lord’s presence 24/7/365 is so much better than what we experience now, the little pockets of heaven here on earth.

Imagine what it will be like, no more separation from the Lord when we depart from this life, but while we wait, we make ourselves content in Him with our eyes on the prize.

title: The Comfort He Gives
verse: "This is my comfort in my affliction: that Your word has revived me." (Psalm 119:50)
content: In our afflictions, what we pray earnestly for, is escape. That is our comfort. God goodness is often weighed by how quickly He brings that escape. God has a purpose for all our afflictions, and often He chooses to give us a different comfort—His Word.  It is packed with promises, exhortations, and lessons we need to learn; God's Word is the most valuable resource that a Christian can have in their life. Are you cherishing and graciously receiving the comfort He gives through His word, or do you keep hoping for an escape from the situations that should bring you closer to God? Let His word revive and comfort you.

title: Sure Foundation
verse: (Isaiah 33:6)“He will be your sure foundation, providing a rich store of salvation, wisdom and knowledge. The fear of the Lord will be your treasure”
content: A prayer in deep distress penned by Isaiah the prophet.A foundation is critical in building a house, it determines the structure.  It is recommended that foundations should only be designed and built by a professional. What an assurance that God is our sure foundation – He is the original designer and definitely the professional.  He takes care of every minute detail.He provides a rich store of salvation, wisdom and knowledge, especially in times of distress. Stand upon this foundation that God has erected and your “building” will never crumble.  It will withstand the tests and storms of life and remain unscathed.

title: Tears
verse: “Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;” - (2 Timothy 1:4)
content:
Paul encourages Timothy to be faithful even when they are apart.

When I was growing up I heard this phrase, absence makes the heart grow weepier and fonder.  It means that when you miss someone you cry because you do not or cannot see them but it also makes you look forward to the time when you will eventually see them.

Our tears do not last forever, thank God for that! It eventually subsides and is replaced with Joy most times.

You may be crying now and your tears seem unending, nothing lasts forever, everything has an expiration date, hold on, joy comes in the morning!

title: Renewed Day by Day
verse: “Therefore we do not lose heart, but though our outer man is decaying, yet our inner man is being renewed day by day.” (1 Corinthians 15:17)
content: Paul tells us that our faith is not in vain; our hope is in the risen Christ. If you are like me, a woman approaching her fifties, you realize that some things are not the same anymore.My vision is no longer 20/20, I have aches and pains and I could go on and on.  And I am sure you have some discoveries of your own.Our faith and hope in God supersedes all that however as our spirit matures, working beyond what our eyes see to make a remarkable eternal impact.  We are being renewed day by day.

title: Created
verse: “So God created man in his [own] image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.” - (Genesis 1:27)
content:
It all began here with the creation story.  God made careful preparation for men and women to inhabit the earth.  He made all necessary provisions before he created mankind.

When you look at yourself you should see the masterpiece that God created – YOU.  He made you in His image so you are like Him, having His capabilities, His power and a heart like His.

Agree with God that you are created in His image and do not limit yourself, doubt your capabilities or question your heart.  Embrace the awesome creature that you are, as you acknowledge the creator.

title: Greatest in the Kingdom
verse: (Matthew 18:4)“Therefore, whoever takes the lowly position of this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.”
content: Jesus’ answer to his disciples asking who is the greatest in the Kingdom.Isn’t it ironic that the world teaches that to be successful you must move up the ranks yet the Kingdom principles teach quite the opposite?In the Kingdom of God success is coupled with humility, service and being lowly. A child is dependent on his or her parents to take care of them; they are simple, carefree, trusting, obedient and full of wonder.The greatest in the Kingdom is the lowly position of a child. It would therefore seem that you and I need to follow suit and take up our positions.

title: Taught by the Holy Spirit
verse: (Luke 12:12)“…for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that time what you should say.”
content: Jesus gives the assurance of the Holy Spirit’s presence when needed.Lisa was short tempered and sass mouth as a teenager but now she is quiet and sweet tempered.  She takes no credit for herself, she’ll tell you that God met her like He did Saul and she had her own Damascus experience.She tells the younger women to display humility.  Be taught by the Holy Spirit she says, He will not let you down and you’ll be safe as long as you follow His instructions.Every situation has a right response and if you listen carefully before you speak, you’ll know how to respond every time.

title: Mercy
verse: “Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.” - (Matthew 5:7)
content:
One of the beatitudes from the Sermon on the Mount that Jesus taught the multitudes.

Forgiveness, kindness and compassion shown to someone when it is within your power to harm them, are expressions of being merciful. In exchange, when you require the same expressions, you too will be shown mercy.

Your supervisor delegates a portion of the task to you to complete, upon review he realizes that you did not follow the instructions implicitly and had he not double checked what you did it would have cost both your jobs. 

He fixed it, showed you the error, no mention of it was made it to the Manager to whom he reports.  Thank God for mercy.

title: Acknowledge Jesus
verse: (Luke 12:8)   “I tell you, whoever publicly acknowledges me before others, the Son of Man will also acknowledge before the angels of God.”
content: Jesus preaches to the multitude giving encouragement.It’s that time of year, the Advent season has begun and Jesus is the reason for the season.  To acknowledge Jesus throughout this season is the thing to do. Without Him where would you be, where would I be?It is worthwhile to know Him and to associate ourselves with Him.  He characterizes every name He is called.You know what He has done in your life and I know what He has done in my life and therefore public acknowledgment is minuscule compared to the magnitude of what He has done and who He has been.

title: Balanced Discipline
verse: "By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD [men] depart from evil.”(Proverbs 16:6)
content: The truth is we’ve all sinned and deserve a penalty. And mercy is that God does not give us the wrathful punishment we deserve. God’s mercy inspires us to turn away from evil and the truth of His Word cleanses us.  Mercy, truth and fear of the Lord combined allow us to depart from wrong doing.God disciplines us in a way that speaks of the true nature of our sin, the mercy of forgiveness, and the desire of wanting to personally depart from evil. Consider your response to God’s discipline in your life. It is because He loves you. Are you responding rightly to it?

title: Count The Cost
verse: "Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. Will he not first sit down and estimate the cost to see if he has enough money to complete it?” - (Luke 14:28)
content:
Luke tells us the cost of being a disciple.

It is practical to evaluate a situation before attempting to tackle it and the cost of an item before purchasing.  Likewise, as followers of Christ, it is practical as well to count the cost of discipleship.

Discipleship requirements include: commitment to the call, dying to self and putting Christ first above people and possessions. When weighed on the scale against the reward, which is eternal life in Christ, it is a small price to pay.

Each person must count the cost and answer the call.

title: Ask for Wisdom
verse: “But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all men generously and without reproach, and it will be given to him.” - (James 1:5)
content:
James implores us to ask for God’s wisdom that will enable us to joyfully go through trials of all kinds.

Wisdom is defined as the quality of having experience, knowledge, and good judgment; the quality of being wise. There are two types of wisdom; intellectual and Godly.  

Godly wisdom comes only from God of course, if you don’t have it, ask for it as directed in this verse.

To overcome trials, we need God’s wisdom which allows us to discern and judge what is the right thing to do in any situation, therefore, let us ask for wisdom.

title: Looking for the way Home
verse: "Jesus said unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no one comes to the Father, but through me.”  (John 14:6)
content: I remember driving with my cousin after she first got her license. We were sleep deprived and had only an outdated road map to guide, so it was no surprise when we accidently found ourselves one highway exit from Toledo, Ohio when we were trying to get to Detroit, Michigan! Sometimes we trust a faulty sense of intuition and an inappropriate guide to get us to Heaven. Jesus assures us that there is only one way—through faith in Him.Affirm your faith in His Word and trust His death on the Cross as the only acceptable payment for sin.

title: Above and Beyond
verse: (Matthew 5:47)"If you greet only your brothers, what are you doing more than others? Do not even pagans do that?"
content: Matthew writes what Jesus says about love for our enemies.Jesus calls us to go above and beyond, to do more than is required, more than is the norm.  This term is usually associated with military personnel but here in this passage it was used when He spoke to the masses; people like you and I.To live and portray Christ-like character is what we are commissioned to do, we ought to take it up a notch and be different from the rest of the world.  Let’s remember that we are the light and the salt of the earth, therefore we go above and beyond.

title: Who is your master?
verse:
“No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve
God and mammon.” - (Matthew 6:24)
content:
Jesus emphasizes faith in God rather than in possessions.

She was between two minds, it’s the weekend but should she go to work to earn some extra cash or stay home, rest and go to church? Evita battled the thoughts in her mind.  More work equals more money; I can go to church anytime she reasoned.

Have you been there?  

The dividing factor is your heart posture; where your loyalties lie and what your motives are. You can have and enjoy both BUT you must serve only one, God.

title: Just Treatment
verse: “But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you, that you may be children of your Father in heaven. He causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.” - (Matthew 5:44-45)
content:
Mathew writes about Jesus’ ministry, these verses addresses love for enemies.

Righteous or not, good or bad God’s provision is just treatment for all.  He unfailingly lavishes oxygen, night and day, sun and rain on all inhabitants of the earth.

Who are we then to be discriminative in the way we treat people in general; friends or enemies? Love for our enemies and prayer for those who persecute us come naturally, when we think of God’s just treatment towards us.

title: The Word of God
verse: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”  (John 1:1)
content: The ancient Greek manuscripts that our English Bibles are derived from use the word Logos for “Word”. It is a word that does not simply mean language or speech, but a deeper, more tangible truth. Logos is the word from which we get our word “logic”. Just as logic is irrefutable, and relevant to every area of life, so is God. Does your life demonstrate the Word of God to the world, or is it weak, lacking personal application, and only manifested in select activities or relationships? Let the Word of God permeate every area of your life for His praise, honor and glory.

title: Where is your Treasure?
verse: “For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.” - (Matthew 6:21)
content:
Jesus makes a profound statement about treasures.
