Abby looked at the things around her, they were many and they were extremely beautiful.  She had married a wealthy man, she lacked nothing.  Her brush with death several years ago changed her focus dramatically, she sees the possessions but they don’t mean much to her.  

Yes she is grateful, but she is more grateful to be alive. Her heart is set on the one who saved and delivered her from death, that’s where her treasure is, not in the things around her.

We can be enticed, so we must keep our hearts focused on Christ the giver of all things. 

Where is your treasure?

title: Throne of Glory
verse: “When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory.” - (Matthew 25:31)
content:
Matthew’s account of the return of Christ in judgment.

Judgment is inevitable but our reaction to it does not have to be fearful.

Jesus will return in glory with the holy angels to sit on the throne of glory.  

For us who have accepted Him as Lord and Savior, it will be a welcoming experience.  For us it means everlasting happiness. He comes in glory, with power and the host of angels with Him to take His rightful place. 

We have nothing to fear, we are his, and He knows us by our names!

title: Crown of Life
verse: “Blessed [is] the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried, he shall receive the crown of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.” - (James 1:12)
content:
James proverbially writes about trials and temptations; how our faith should work throughout trials.

When Anna was going through the temptation of having a relationship with a married man, she felt anything but blessed.  Today she stands at the front of the room as she ministers to other women about how she overcame that temptation and encourages them that they can too.

She proudly wears her crown of life, after she’s been tried.

Regardless of the temptations that you are facing right now, you can and will overcome to receive your crown of life.

title: The Topaz of Tribulation
verse: “But as for you, ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good, to bring to pass, as it is this day, to save much people alive.”  (Genesis 50:20)
content: Women who have had to live in an abusive relationship, endure many difficult trials through no fault of their own. They should not hold themselves responsible for the irrational actions of others. Spiteful actions, family turning against them, torment, and the love they once cherished stripped away as they become mere objects. And yet, God can use these horrible situations to bring good and to save many others. Women in these positions understand the sufferings and can pull others through their darkest hours. They’ve developed sensitivity and compassion which can be used to help others.

title: Working for Grace?
verse: “Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.”  (Matthew 11:28)
content: God is gracious to all especially to the weary. Those who come to Him wearied, burdened, and tired of climbing uphill, He promises to give rest. The fear of doing wrong can make us weary and burdensome. We sometimes put our trust in our own efforts rather than in Christ’s. Our own efforts will never produce the rest we desire. Ephesians 2:8 says, “For by grace are ye saved through faith: and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God.” Christ paid the penalty for sin on Calvary therefore we approach Him in humility.If you are weary and burdened, Christ will give you rest.

title: Forgiveness
verse: (Matthew 6:14)“For if you forgive other people when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.”
content: A portion of the Lord’s Prayer that Jesus gave to us.Forgiveness is the action or process of forgiving or being forgiven, releasing negative thoughts such as resentment and bitterness and the desire for revenge.  Similar words are: pardon, absolution, exoneration, mercy, pit and leniency.  Forgiveness is liberating act.  It is comes highly commanded by God, who says, “If you forgive others then I will also forgive you”.It’s not worth it to abhor un-forgiveness; you are the only being hurt and stifled by that action. Regardless of the offence committed against you, give up the desire to execute judgment because forgiveness of others equals forgiveness from God.

title: Bow Down
verse: (Psalm 72:11)“And let all kings bow down before him, All nations serve him.”
content: The Psalmist writes about the glory of the Messiah’s reign. The adoration continues, let all nations and people bow down and serve the King of Kings, the Lord Jesus Christ.Silvia knew what it meant to have people honor her, her prestigious job and title afforded her such luxury but she only allowed because it was protocol.  She worked in the military. She honors the Lord Jesus Christ and she’ll tell you that nothing else is more important and that nothing else matters to her. It is before Him she bows down.Bow down and worship Him today and always, He is the one true and living God who deserves to honored.

title: Joy and Gladness
verse: (Luke 1:14)“And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth”
content: The birth of John the Baptist foretold.As a mother I quite understand the joys (after the pains of labor) experienced of having a child.  Some mothers vow never again only to be seen years after giving birth yet again. John the Baptist is known as the precursor of Jesus, he was his cousin but he also had a mandate to prepare the way for him.It is recorded that because of his birth there will be joy and gladness.You and I can like John prepare the way for Jesus everywhere we go, in so doing there will be rejoicing, joy and gladness.Tell others about Jesus.

title: Teacher and Lord
verse: (John 13:13)“You call Me Teacher and Lord; and ye say well; for so I Am.”
content: John recounts Jesus washing His disciples’ feet and His discourse with them.With her head facing the rising sun Maya was filled with joy as she retraced the course of her life.  She wasn’t always so content. She had grown up with a father who did not know how to love and as a result she was deficient in many areas of her life.  But she met the Saviour who has since become her teacher and Lord.By the power of the Holy Spirit she is taught and is keen to obey His words acknowledging Him as Lord of her life. Will you make Him teacher and Lord of your life too?

title: Steadfast
verse: “God [is] in the midst of her; she shall not be moved: God shall help her, [and that] right early.” - (Psalms 46:5)
content:
The Psalmist speaks of God, the refuge of his people; God helping at the break of dawn.

You are doubled over in pain and you can’t move, the phone rings and you barely stretch to answer it.  It’s your girlfriend, she’s checking in to see how you are doing.  Through sobbing and tears you tell her what has been happening and before you can put the phone down she’s at your house.

That is God in the midst of you, sending help to you.  He is steadfast; unwavering, resolute, committed when it pertains to you and your wellbeing.  You will not be moved.

title: Honest Earnings
verse: “Dishonest money dwindles away, but he who gathers money little by little makes it grow.” - (Proverbs 13:11)
content:
Wisdom loves righteousness; Solomon declares this in this chapter.

Every day the mothers in the church put aside a small portion of money from their income.  This practice has developed and continued for many years, faithfully they have sacrificed. 

They have not gambled, or tried to extort money under false pretenses; they have worked with their hands, doing extra and menial tasks, making honest earnings.

It does not seem feasible what they have accomplished from those savings. Children have gone to college, houses and cars have been bought, donations made to charities, medical bills paid.  Little by little it grew, until it became enormous and sufficient for the need; the power diligence.

title: Fulfill the Law
verse: “Owe no man anything, but to love one another: for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law.” - (Romans 13:8)
content:
The Romans are encouraged to put on Christ, obey authority and love their neighbor. In context, this passage refers to paying honor, respect and taxes. 

Love for others is not optional; it is likened to a debt owed that must be paid and in doing so we fulfill the law.  A key Christian principle, is being a lawful citizen; paying our bills and taxes.

Regardless of who the person is, what they have done, what they can or cannot do for us, our only obligation is to love each other.

Mary, Heather, the mad woman, the Government minister, we will love and thus fulfill the law.

title: The Lord's
verse: “The earth [is] the LORD'S, and the fullness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.” - (Psalm 24:1)
content:
The Psalmist writes about the King of Glory and His Kingdom.

The earth, everything and everyone in it belongs to the Lord. From the smallest to the largest creature, the poorest to the richest person, known or unknown,  the ugliest to the most beautiful landscape, the smallest brook to the largest ocean, the best or the worst day, hidden or seen, it all belongs to Him.

It’s not difficult to see that everything points to the Lord, He knows about it all.  

Let us therefore give Him praise and use what He has made available to us to the glory and honor of the Lord’s name.

title: Treasure
verse: "The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and then in his joy went and sold all he had and bought that field.” - (Matthew 13:44)
content:
Jesus tells the parable of the hidden treasure.

You went to the store, saw something that is quite unusual and you wanted to get it but you didn’t have enough money.  You went to the store owner, paid what you had and asked her to put it aside until you will return in a few days with the balance to take it away.

We ladies can relate to that!

So too is the Kingdom of heaven, a treasure which we work at possessing because it is worth every effort that we make.

title: Bound by Selfishness, Freed by Sacrifice
verse: “Through him everyone who believes is set free from every sin, a justification you were not able to obtain under the law of Moses.” (Acts 13:39)
content: Freedom is a wonderful thing to have and to celebrate. But are you celebrating freedom from sin, or freedom to sin? Although it may seem that freedom is having the chance to make choices, in fact, having the “freedom to sin” so to speak, is not freedom at all. It is bondage. The book of Romans teaches that, to whom or whatever you yield yourself to and obey you are in bondage to it. Yield yourself to God in faith and obedience; justification can only be attained through Christ alone who gives you true freedom.

title: A Choice to Make
verse: “[It is] better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.” - (Proverbs 21:19)
content:
This is a reference made of a married woman and whom she ought not to be.
 
Married or not, being an angry woman destroys our relationships.  We have a choice to make; will we deal with our anger issues or allow them to destroy our relationships? 

To say that it is better to live in the wilderness than to live with an angry woman speaks for itself, - living in isolation and hardship rather than with contention.

The choice to make then is to deal with our anger, so that we can be peaceful and joyful and a blessing to interact with.

title: Gain
verse: “For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.” - (Philippians 1:21)
content:
Paul’s indication that a Christian’s joy is even more solid when based upon the realization that God is stronger than death. 

To die is gain? 

To die is gain from the perspective of a life in Christ who gives everlasting life.

Both physical death and dying to one’s self are gainful, we decrease and Christ increases.  When we are alive, we live for Christ and when we die we will be in eternity with Him.

The words for today: live everyday for Christ, exhibiting Godly qualities, making an impact. When death comes, think of it as a promotion, it is not the end, just the beginning.

To die is GAIN!

title: New Generation
verse: “Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation; the old has gone, the new has come!  All this is from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and gave us the ministry of reconciliation:” - (2 Corinthians 5:17-18)
content:
Chapter five is titled “Paul’s Hope of Eternal Glory.” 

I recently heard the story of a man who suffered a massive heart attack and was pronounced dead.  But God restored his life. Sometime afterward his blood work test reveled that he had a new DNA which showed no connection to having had a heart attack.

That’s what God does, makes us new creations. When we think of His goodness towards us, it prompts us to restore and maintain friendly relations with others.

title: Blessed
verse: “Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked or stand in the way that sinners take or sit in the company of mockers” - (Psalms 1:1)
content:
The Psalmist gives instructions for righteous living.

Which do you want? Blessing or curse? 

Blessing leads to happiness and righteous living while curse leads to unhappiness and a wicked lifestyle.  

To be called blessed you must avoid that which is evil.  Evil works, evil company, evil deeds, and evil speech must be shunned. 

Blessed is different and it demands a different lifestyle.  Do not be pressured by the way of the world which strives with the way of God.  Walk the righteous path and be called blessed to the honor and glory of your Father above.

title: Fame of the Humble
verse: “Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.”  (Matthew 5:5)
content: Meekness/humility is a difficult attribute for many women to comprehend. Meekness does not mean that you intentionally portray a poor appearance or that you talk poorly about yourself.  The difficultly in understanding this comes because emphasis is placed on the physical and not the spiritual. Meekness is a lifestyle of putting other people above self. Jesus’ promise of giving the earth to the meek for an inheritance is a promise. If we spend our life actively serving others, instead of trying to gain power by climbing on or over them and satisfying our desires for attention or popularity, then we have that promise to claim as ours. That is the fame of the humble.

title: Living Hope
verse: “Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,” - (1 Peter 1:3)
content:
The Apostle Paul writes about growth of Christian character.

When I think of the term “living hope”, I think of something perpetual. 

God be praised for His great mercy which affords us new life through the resurrection of Jesus from the dead.

It all hinges on the fact that Jesus not only died but rose from the dead; He defeated death.  

Each day is filled with its own baggage of stress, depression and anxiety; but thanks be to God, we are born again into a perpetual hope because Jesus conquered it all.

title: Thanksgiving and Praise!
verse: (Psalms 105:1-2)“Oh give thanks to the LORD; call upon his name; make known his deeds among the peoples! Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works!”
content: The everlasting faithfulness of God is outlined in this Psalm.It’s New Year’s Eve; a good time for thanksgiving and praise! As we reflect on our lives this past year we have much to give thanks and praise for.  Beginning the New Year is ideal for thanksgiving and praise as well.Resoundingly let us give thanks to God, call upon His name and let others know what He has done in our lives.  Sing your song of adoration, one year ends and another begins.God is good all the time and all the time God is good.

title: Meaningless
verse: “Whoever loves money never has money enough; whoever loves wealth is never satisfied with his income. This too is meaningless.” - (Ecclesiastes 5:10)
content:
“Riches are meaningless,” declares Solomon.

Things such as money are relatively meaningless if your focus is on excessive love, consuming and creating more and more of it.

If you are sick, money will not cure you, and if you are lonely, it will not comfort you. 

Money has its place and value and helps in various situations but it is not everything.  Running it down will not solve your problems, your appetite will increase without satisfaction.  

Meaningless; empty, of no significance, pointless, has no purpose, worthless … got it? There is so much more to life that is of value and worth, stay clear from that which is meaningless.

title: Light and Salvation
verse: “The LORD is my light and my salvation— whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life— of whom shall I be afraid?” (Psalm 27:1)
content: A joyful declaration of faith by the Psalmist.As children, most persons were terrified of the dark and some of us took it into adulthood with us.  Light illuminates darkness and that is what the Lord does for us.  He is my light and salvation, not only does His light shine but He also saves, so that we need not be afraid of people or be fearful in general.What are you afraid of?  Who terrifies you?  Apply the light and salvation to it. It will shine if it was dark and be saved if it was lost.

title: Promise for the Persecuted
verse: “Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.” (Matthew 5:10)
content: Injustice is not easy to endure peaceably. To be denied the opportunity to testify of your own innocence is a hurtful embittering experience, more so without Christ. With Christ however, persecution can bring us great joy. 1 Peter 4:14 says that, persecution is a rewarding experience because God’s glory and spirit rests upon you. If you find yourself in a situation where you are called to endure injustice for Christ’s sake, utilize the experience well.  Do not seek escape from the trials that beset you or exhibit bitterness.The kingdom of heaven is the promised for the persecuted, including you! You are blessed, be strong!

title: Sacred Rest
verse: “Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.” (Exodus 20:8)
content: God established for us a period of rest and work when He created the world in six days and rested on one. So too our lives should be marked by this pattern. For mothers and wives, getting rest can become almost impossible; spills to be cleaned, food to be served, and others to be cared every day of the week. How then can we honor this command to rest from our works and to rest in Christ?  We can honor it by using the day of rest to commit ourselves and our families to reflection and study of God’s words, and by ceasing from physical work and activities that shift our focus from Him. Scared rest is possible.

title: From Darkness to Light
verse: “to open their eyes so that they may turn from darkness to light and from dominion of Satan to God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins and an inheritance among those who have been sanctified by faith in Me.” (Acts 26:18)
content: Paul is commissioned to bring this good news to the Jews and Gentiles of his day.We do it every day when we awake; we open our eyes to see, from the “darkness” of slumber to the “light” of day - an automatic action but also an act of faith.  It ushers us towards new possibilities each day.  So too Christ made provision for us by His death on the cross to come out of the darkness of sin into the light of salvation.

title: Not Angels
verse: “But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?” - (Hebrews 1:13)
content:
The Son of God is esteemed above angels.

Think about it, a footstool is placed under your feet when you sit in a chair; so if the enemies of the Son of God will be made His footstool that means that they will be subdued.

We are hid with God in Christ and it is His enemies that will be subdued.  Because we are hid in Christ our enemies are also subdued.  Whoever contends with us contends with God and is brought to naught.

It is not angels, but the Son of God who triumphs over the enemy on our behalf.

title: One in Christ
verse: "There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus." - (Galatians 3:28)
content:
Is it Faith or Law? : Paul writes to the people of Galatia, who were insistent on Jewish laws, it is justification by faith.

Has anyone ever made you feel special? Think back at how it made you feel, it did not matter that you were female, they were nice to you and it made you feel loved and appreciated.  

So too in Christ we are special: everyone is, nothing changes that, not your height, the color of your eyes, NOTHING.  It is not about the rules, by faith we are one in Christ.

title: Eternal Life
verse: “Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life; whoever does not obey the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.” - (John 3:36)
content:
John the Baptist’s testimony about Jesus.

Belief in the Son is to eternal life, as disobedience to the Son is to judgment; there are no two ways about it.  

God is loving and kind but He is also just, therefore He must judge sin and disobedience.  Acceptance of His Son affords us the opportunity to avoid God’s wrath.   

Today get in the safe zone of eternal life and at all cost avoid the danger zone of God’s judgment.  

God desires that none perish, but that all experience eternal life through repentance. (2 Peter 3:9).  

Make the choice of eternal life.

title: Not by Works
verse: “Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;” - (Titus 3:5)
content:
Paul’s epistle to Titus regarding the theology of righteous living.

No matter how good you are, how hard you work or how “righteous” you are, you must accept Christ as Lord and Savior.  It is not by works of righteousness that salvation comes. 

You are saved according to His mercy, so stop wearing yourself out trying to be righteous, because that’s not how you get acceptance from God.

Accepting Him by faith and yielding to the Holy Spirit is how the journey starts; simple and uncomplicated.  

Rest in the assurance that God’s mercy supersedes “works of righteousness”.

title: Bearing with One Another
verse: “Be completely humble and gentle; be patient, bearing with one another in love.  Make every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit through the bond of peace.” - (Ephesians 4:2-3)
content:
This is a sure recipe for unity in the body of Christ.

Oh it is so easy to become puffed up with pride and impatience, after going through a situation and having mastered it, becoming intolerant with someone presently going through the same situation.

But for unity to reign, we must be humble, gentle, and patient in love, so that peace may abound.

It is always useful to remember where we have journeyed from, how long it took us, and what we learnt along the way. This attitude will help us to be tolerant.

title: Pearls of Spiritual Purity
verse: "Do not give what is holy to dogs, and do not throw your pearls before swine,” (Matthew 7:6)
content: Matthew records the things that counts with God as spoken by Jesus.Because women are relationship-driven, pleasing the people in their lives is of high priority. And so, for the sake of building relationships, many women make themselves vulnerable to sin. God has set you apart—He desires you to be holy! This great jewel that He has given you must be safeguarded. “Guard your heart with all diligence, for out of it flow the issues of life.” - Proverbs 4:23.Do not settle for less than what God has ordained for you, you are worth every bit of it.  Do not give yourself away worthlessly.

title: Feast of the Hungry
verse: “For He has satisfied the thirsty soul, and the hungry soul He has filled with what is good.” (Psalm 107:9)
content: Moms know that the cries of a baby’s hunger will not be satisfied by anything but milk. A pacifier may work for a while but that baby is going to let you know that he or she is hungry! If you are breastfeeding, your baby knows that no one else will be able to nurture and meet his or her needs. Likewise, we who desire to be forgiven of our sin know that nothing else will suffice; good works may ease our conscience a little, like a pacifier, but it will not give us peace with God. If we will hunger for Him, He will feed us.

title: The Source of Soundness
verse: "Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.” (Matthew 4:4)
content: Where does your understanding of God from? Your beliefs about who He is? Can they be explained explicitly from Scripture?     For many women theology comes from a coffee cup, a greeting card, a calendar, a movie, a book, or sometimes from another religion. When Jesus said that we are to live by every word that comes from the mouth of God, He meant it! Our understanding of God and the world should come from the source who is Truth himself. Surrender to the authority of God’s Word which is the source of all truth and garner all of your thinking from it.

title: Wear the Belt
verse: “Stand firm therefore, having girded your loins with truth,…”   (Ephesians 6:14)
content: The armor of God consists of different pieces of garments which are for our ultimate protection. In this first verse, truth is compared to a belt. A belt has the function of stabilizing a garment; it holds it in a certain position. Truth must be what we stabilize our faith with. We cannot stabilize our faith with feeling, experience, charitable deeds, or another person’s testimony. We must stabilize our faith with Truth. Scripture teaches that Jesus is the very embodiment of Truth: He displays and portrays the standard and definition — in fact, in John 14:6, He says, “I am the Way, the Truth, and the Life.” Are you standing firm and your loins girded with this truth?

title: Beware
verse: “And he said unto them, take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.” - (Luke 12:15)
content:
The parable of the rich fool, Jesus warns about putting faith in money.

The middle aged woman looking out the window of her limousine was very wealthy, whatever she desired she bought; money was not an issue.  She had worked hard to amass her wealth.  

Jennifer was not happy however and she was lonely.  

She will tell you not to follow in her footsteps, she has leant that there are many things so much more important than money.

“Beware”, she warns,” live a balanced life; build relationships with people and not things.”

Take this advice to build a balanced life.

title: Unconditional Love
verse: "The Lord appeared to him from afar, saying, “I have loved you with an everlasting love; Therefore I have drawn you with lovingkindness.“ (Jeremiah 31:3)
content: The words, "I love you" lose their meaning when our actions don't follow. Love is not an emotion, it is an action. 1 Corinthians 13 gives a list of loving actions. It goes a step further; it suggests that true love outlasts all positive emotion, all good days, and even the way you have been treated. True love is an everlasting decision to sacrifice, forgive, and kindess. Is your love toward the world, and your family more specifically, truly unconditional, or is it removed by your emotions? Just as the Lord loves you, so too, you must endeavor to love others.

title: According to his Purpose
verse: (Romans 8:28)“And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.”
content: Paul records that even difficult things and situations can be used in God’s overall plan for good.The little girl sat and labored tirelessly over the pieces of puzzle before her; she will not sleep a wink until she has put them all into place because she is curious to see the finished picture.So too we labor over our lives trying in earnest to fix the pieces like a puzzle. The master of the puzzle looks on smiling because He knows that regardless and in spite of, all the pieces will fit together according to His purpose.

title: Created Equal
verse: “There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female: for you are all one in Christ Jesus.”  (Galatians 3:28)
content: This verse does not mean that gender has no value to God or that there is no difference in the ministries of male and female. Rather, it means that there is no difference in the spiritual value or capacity of either. Regardless of our nationality, background, economic status, or gender, Jesus equally saves us, equally offers fellowship, knowledge, and virtue to everyone. Although women were created as helpmates they are not inferior. Their virtue, knowledge, salvation, and fellowship with God are equal to that of their male counterparts. God intended women to be appreciated the way He created them.

title: Ignoring the Warning
verse: “If I should say, “My foot has slipped,” your loving kindness O Lord will hold me up.”  (Psalm 94:18)
content: The Psalmist describes God as the refuge of the righteous.   God’s gentle warnings of our need for repentance can go ignored. We continue in our sin until we reach a point where we think, “I’ve gone too far and I can never get back.” Getting back into fellowship with God is possible through Jesus Christ. The Bible says that He ever lives to make intercession for us perpetually in Heaven – Hebrews 7:25. We may slip, but underneath us are His everlasting arms – Deuteronomy 33:27. God’s mercy holds us together as a thread does fabric and keeps us from failing and falling, therefore don’t ignore the warning!

title: In The Light
verse: “He that saith he is in the light, and hateth his brother, is in darkness even until now.” - (1 John 2:9)
content:
If we know Christ, His ways and commands, then we will walk in the light.

An amazing fact about light; a space is classified as dark because light is only visible when it has an object to bounce off.

We are such an object in Christ.  We will remain in the light by loving others and dispelling darkness.

Visible light illuminates, reflects, and refracts.  We too as objects of light, point and lead others to Christ.  We refuse to be in darkness, we are delighted to act as rainbows that are colorful and represent a promise to people everywhere we shine.

title: Limits of Prayer
verse: “This is the confidence which we have before in Him, that, if we ask anything according to his will, He hears us.”  (1 John 5:14)
content: There are only two parameters to our prayers: God’s will and our faith. Keeping God’s will in mind, should guide us to ask for things only according to His will. With faith in mind, we should keep asking without ceasing, until the prayer is answered. How can we be certain that we are praying according to His will? Praying His Word is one way. Studying God’s Word to understand His purpose is another way. As we pray about a need, let’s surrender our hearts to accept God’s way of meeting the need. He will meet our needs according to His riches in glory.
